004.title: A:Oh,I've  left  the  lights  on  again.
004.explanation:
●     I     can't     understand     why     he's     being      so     selfish.He     isn't     usually     like      that.
(being     selfish      表示此时此刻行为自私)
但 是 ● He   never   thinks   about   other   people.He   is   very   selfish. ( 而非he   is   being)
(他平常都很自私，不仅仅表现在此时此刻)
用am/is/are being 来表示人的行为，在下列句子中通常不能使用进行时：
● It's  hot  today. (而非it  is  being  hot)               ● Sarah  is  very  tired. (而非is   being   tired) look  与feel比较
一 般 现 在 时 或 现 在 进 行 时 可 以 用 来 表 示 现 在 的 表 情 或 感 觉 ：
● You      look      well     today.   或   You're          looking           welltoday.
●  How  do  you  feel  now?   或 How  are  you  feeling  now?
但是 ● Iusually  feel  tired   in  the  morning.( 而非I'm   usually   feeling)
现在进行时与一般现在时比较(1) → UNT 3
现在时表示将来 → UNM119
UNIT
EXERCISES

005.title: ……………………(you/listen)to      the      radio       every      day?"'No,just      occasionally.'
005.explanation:
1  I  can't  understand  why   he’s  bein..  so  selfish.He isn't usually like that.
2   Jack                  ………………………very nice to me at the moment.I wonder why.
3 You'll like Jill when you meet her.She                                             very nice.
4 Normally you are very sensible,so why                                …ssilly    bout   this   matter?
5 Why isn't Sarah at work today? … … … … … … … … … … … … …          …ill?
UNIT
5 一般过去时(I did)
请看下列例句：
动词一般过去时是在原形动词后加-ed  ( 规则动词):
●I work in a travel agency now.Before that I worked in a shop.
·We invited them to our party but they decided not to come.
● The police stopped me on my way home last night.
●She passed her examination because she studied very hard. (stopped,    studied    等 )拼写法参见附录6
但许多动词的过去式是不规则的，过去式不在原形后加-ed:
write  →    wrote                  ·Mozart    wrote    more    than     600     pieces     of    music.
·We    saw   Rose    in   town    a    few    days    ago.
● I went to the cinema three times last week.
● It was cold,so I shut the window. 不规则动词表参见附录1
一般过去时疑问句和否定句中使用did/didn't+    动词原形：
·A:Did  you  go  out  last  night?
B:Yes,I went to the cinema but I didn't enjoy the film much.
·'When did Mr Thomas die?’‘About ten years ago.'
● They didn't invite her to the party,so she didn't go.
●   'Did   you   have   time   to   write   the   letter?''No,I   didn't.' 注意在下面的例句中do 是主要动词：
●What  did  you  do   at  the  weekend?(而非what  did  you  at  the  weekend)
·I    didn't   do anything.  ( 而非I   didn't   anything) be(am/is/are  )的过去式是was/ were:
I/he/she/it was/wasn't        was I/he/she/it?
we/you/they were/weren't     were  we/you/they?
was/were在否定句和疑问句中不使用did:
●I was angry  because  they were late.
Was  the  weather  good  when  you  were  on  holiday?
● They weren't able to come because they were so busy.
·Did you go out last night or were you too tired?
一般过去时与过去进行时比较 → UWIT 6 一般过去时与现在完成时比较 → UMITS 12-14

UNIT
I was doing             I finished doing
过去                       现在
● This time last year I was living in Brazil.
● What were you doing at 10 o'clock last night?
● I waved to her but she wasn't looking.
一般过去时经常与过去进行时配合使用，表示动作发生在另一动作的进行中：
● Tom burnt his hand when he was cooking the dinner.
·Isaw you in the park yesterday.You were sitting on the grassand reading a book.
·While I was working in the garden ,I hurt my back.
但是一般过去时也用来说明动作发生的前后顺序：
●  Iwas  walking  along  the  road  when  I  saw  Dave.So  I  stopped  and  we  had  a  chat. 请比较：
有些动词(如know  /want/believe)    一般不能用进行时的形式(参见第4单元 A 节):
● We   were   good    friends.We   knew each other well.(而非we were  knowing)
●I  was  enjoying  the  party  but  Chris  wanted to go home. ( 而非was   wanting)

现在完成时(1)(I have done)
请看下列例句：

He  has  lost  his  key ( 他把钥匙丢了并仍未找到) have/has     lost 是现在完成时的构成形式：
现在完成时由have/has    + 过去分词构成  规则动词的过去分词是在不定式后加-ed,      但许多有用的动词 是不规则的(lost/done/been/written     等 )  有关不规则动词请参见附录1
现在完成时总是与现在有密切的联系，表示过去的动作现在有了结果：
●   'Where's    your    key?''I    don't    know. I've  lost  it.'(我现在仍未找到)
·He  told  me  his  name  but  I've   forgotten    it. (我现在已经不记得了)
●  'Is   Sally  here?'      'No  ,she's    gone    out.’(她现在已经不在了)
●  I   can't   find  my  bag.Have  you  seen  it?( 你知道在哪儿吗?) 现在完成时常用于提供新的信息或告知最近发生的事情：
● Ow!I've cut my finger.
● The road is closed.There's been (=there has been)an accident.
● (新闻报道)The  police  have  arrested  two  men  in  connection  with  the  robbery.
C    与just,already      和yet  连用的现在完成时：
注意gone   (to )和 been   (to) 的区别：
●Jim   is   away    on   holiday. He  has  gone  to   Spain.  ( 他现在或在西班牙，或在去西班牙的路上)
·Jane   is   back    home   from    holiday   now. She   has  been  to  Italy. (她已经从意大利回来了) been   (to)  的用法另参见第8单元和第125单元B 节

13 请成时(I have done)与一般过去时(I did)比较(1)
十分钟后：
现在完成时总是用来讲述现在发生的动作或行为。 Tom  has   lost  his  key 表示他现在没有钥匙(参见 第7单元)。
一般过去时只讲述过去发生的动作。如有人说 Tom     lost    his    key,这并不表明汤姆现在是否有钥匙， 只说明他在过去某个时候把钥匙丢了。
再看两个例句：
● Jack  grew  a  beard  but  now  he  has   shaved  it  off.  (他现在没有胡须)
● They  went  out  after  lunch  and  they've  just   come  back. (他们现在回来了)
如果动作与现在没有联系(如： 一段时间前发生的动作),不使用现在完成时：
● The Chinese invented printing.(而非have invented)
·How    many     plays     did    Shakespeare    write?(而非has      Shakespeare      written)
● Beethoven was a  great composer.     (  而非has      been) 请比较：
●  Shakespeare wrote many plays.
·My   sister   is   a   writer.She   has  written  many  books.  (她仍在写书)
现在完成时用于提供新的信息(参见第7单元)。但如果继续谈下去， 一般需使用一般过去时：
● A:Ow! I've  burnt myself.
B:How did you do that?(而非have you done)
A:I  picked    up  a  hot  dish. (而非have      picked)
●A:Look!Somebody has spilt milk on the carpet.
B:Well,it  wasn't  me.I  didn't  do  it. ( 而非hasn’t     been...haven’t     done)
A:I  wonder  who  it  was  then. (而非who  it  has  been)

have 与 have got
have  与 have   got(拥有)
have  常与 got   一起使用，而不是单独使用.可以说：
● We'v e got a new car.      或  We have a new car.
●  Ann  has  got  two   sisters.       或     Ann   has   two   sisters. 用 have   got 或 have    表示疼痛和患有疾病：
● Ive got a headache.      或  I have a headache. 下面三种形式可以用在疑问句和否定句中：

● Ann had long fair hair,didn't she?
have breakfast / have a bath /have a good time 等

● Goodbye!I hope you have a nice time.
● Mary had a baby recently.
have  got   不能用在下面的表达法中。比较：
● Iusually have a sandwich for my lunch.(have表示“吃饭”,而非have got) 但是 ●  I've got some sandwiches. Would you like one?
在下面的例句中，have  是普通动词，可以使用进行时形式( is      having/ are    having等):
● I had a postcard from Fred this morning.He's on holiday.He says he's having a wonderful time. (而非he has a wonderful time)
● The   phone  rang  while  we  were  having  dinner. ( 而非while   we    had) 一般在疑问句和否定句中用do/does/did:
● I  don't  usually  have  a  big breakfast.   ( 而非I    usually  haven't)
● What  time  does  Ann  have lunch?  ( 而非has   Ann   lunch)

UNIT
18   used to (do)
请看下列句子：
Something used to happen  意思是说过去经常发生，但现在不再发生了：
● I used to play tennis  a lot but I don't play very often now.
● Diane  used  to  travel  a  lot.These  days  she  doesn't  go  away  so  often.
● 'Do   you    go   to    the    cinema   very    often?''Not     now, but   I   used   to.’(=I   used   to   go...) 用used    to 表示过去发生而现在已经不存在的事情或习惯：
● This building is now a furniture shop.It used to be a cinema.
● I used to think he was unfriendly but now I realise he's a very nice person.
● I've started drinking coffee recently.I never used to like it before.
● Janet  used  to  have  very  long  hair  when   she  was   a  child.
I used to  do     something  仅用于一般过去时，不能说 I   use   to   do。表示现在的习惯，要用一般现在时 (I   do)。 请比较：
过去      he used to smoke          we used to live         there  used  to be
现在      he smokes                   we live                    there is
● We used to live in a small village but now we live in London.
● There used to be four cinemas in the town.Now there is only one.
疑问句形式是did  (you)  use to ...?:
● Did you use to eat a lot of sweets when you were a child?
否定形式是didn't  use  to..  (used  not  to.. 也可以用):
● I didn't use to like him.(或I used not to  like  him.)
请比较I  used   to   do  与 I   was    doing  (参见第6单元):
● I   used   to   watch   TV   a   lot.   (  过去我常看电视，但现在不再常看电视了)
● I was   watching    TV    when   the    phone    rang.  ( 我正在看电视)
不要混淆I  used  to  do   与 I  am  used  to  doing的用法(参见第60单元),它们的形式与意义是不同的：
● I  used  to  live   alone.  (过去我独自生活，但现在不再独自生活了)
● I  am  used  to   living  alone. (我已独自生活一段时间，我习惯了，因此我并不认为独自生活新奇)
36        would(=used to) → UNIT 400       be/get used to (doing)something→ UNIT 60

UNIT
23  I  will与 I'm going to 比较
将来的动作
请看will   与 going   to 的区别：
请比较
·‘George phoned while you were  out.'‘OK.I'lphone him back.'
●  ‘George  phoned  while  you  were  out.''Yes,I  know.I'm  going  to  phone  him  back.'
●  'Ann  is  in  hospital.'Oh  really?I  didn't  know.Illgo  and  visit  her.' 但是 ·‘Ann  is  in  hospital.'‘Yes,I know.I'm going to visit her tomorrow.
将来发生的事或情景(推测未来)
有时 will   与going    to 没有什么区别：
● I think the weather will be nice later.
● I think the weather is going to be nice later.
something    is   going  to  happen  用于表示在现在的情况下已经知道(或想到)的动作或行为：
● Look at those black clouds.It's going to rain. (而非it  will  rain—we  can   see  the clouds  now)
●    Ifeel    terrible.I    think  I'm  going to be  sick.  (  而非I     think     I'llbe     sick-Ifeel     terrible now  )在这些情况下不用will 。( 另参见第20单元C 节)
在其他的情况下，用will  要安全些：
● Tom will probably arrive at about 8 o'clock.
●I think Ann will like the present we bought for her.

can, could 与 (be)able to比较
用can  表示可能的事或一个人的能力，形式是 can+  动词不定式(can    do/can     see 等):
● We can see the lake from our bedroom window.
● Can you speak any foreign languages?
● I can come and see you tomorrow if you like.
否定形式是 ca n't (= cannot):
● I'm afraid I can't come to the party on Friday.
(be)able     to.. 可以代替 can,  但是can 更常用：
● Are you able to speak any foreign languages?
但是can,    只有两种形式 can   ( 现在时形式)与could    ( 过去时形式)因此有时需要用(be)able         to.. 请比较：

●  Tom  can  come  tomorrow.
但是： Tom might be  able  to  come  tomorrow.(can没有不定式形式)
could 与was able to...比较
有时 could 是 can   的过去式，特别是与下面的词一起用时：
remember       understand
·When we went into the house,we could smellburning.
●  She  spoke  in  a  very  low  voice,but  I  could  understand  what  she  said. could    也用来表示一般具有的能力或允许做某事：
●My  grandfather  could  speak  five  languages.
● We  were  completely  free. We could do what we wanted. (我们被允许做……)
否定式couldn't (could not)可以用于所有情况：
●My  grandfather  couldn't  swim.
●We tried hard but we couldn't persuade them to come with us.
● Alf played well but he couldn't beat Jack.

027.title: could    (do)与 could  have  (done)比较 must  与 can't   比较
027.explanation:
1 My grandfather was a very clever man.He  .could.  speak five languages.
2 I looked everywhere for the book but I ..couldn't.  find it.
3 They didn't want to come with us at first but we …were able to   persuade  them.
4 Laura had hurt her leg and………………………………………walk very well.
5 Sue wasn't at home when Iphoned but ………………………………………       contact her at her office.
6 I looked very carefully and ……………………………………………     se a figure in the distance.
7 I wanted to buy some tomatoes .The  first  shop I went to didn't have any but I …  ……………………  …get some in the next shop.
8My   grandmother loved music.She …       ……………………… play the piano very well.
9 A girl fell into the river but fortunately we ……………………………………………rescue her.
10 I had forgotten to bring my camera so              …………………………… take any photographs.
以上句中用can  也可以( W e  can  go  for  a   walk    等 )   could    不像can    那么肯定、如果讲的不是真话， 必须用could   . 不 能 用can:
● I'm so angry with him. I could kill him!(而非I  can  killhim)
could  还用来表示在现在或将来动作发生的可能性：
● The phone is ringing.It could be Tim.
●  I  don't  know  when  they'llbe  here.They   could   arrive  at  any  time.
在这些例句中不能用can (不能说 It  can  be  Tim)
在这些句子中could  与 might  是一样的(参见第29-30单元):
● The phone is ringing.It might be Tim.
请比较 could  (do)与 could     have(done):
● I'm   so   tired.I   could   sleep for   a   week.  (  现在)
● I was so tired. I could have slept for a week. ( 过去) could have(done)  常用于表示可能但却没有发生的动作或事情：
●  Why  did  you  stay  at  a  hotel  when  you  went  to  New  York?You  could  have  stayed  with Barbara.   ( 你有机会同她在一起但你没有同她在一起)
● Jack felloff a ladder yesterday but he's all right.He's lucky-he could have hurt himself badly. ( 但他没受伤)
● The situation was bad but it could have been worse.
有时could  的意思是 would    be   able  to...:
● We  could  go  away  if we  had  enough  money.(我们可以去)
● I don't know how you work so hard.I couldn't do it.
could  have(done)=would  have  been  able  to  (do):
●Why didn't Liz apply for the job?She could have got it.
●We could have gone away if we'd had enough money.
● The trip was cancelled last week.Paul couldn't have gone anyway because he was ill. (他不会去)
● You  did  very  well  to  pass  the  exam.I'm    sure   I   couldn't   have   passed   it. (如果我参加 我不会通过)
couldn't have (done)→  UNIT 28B       c ould 与 might比较 → could  与 it  连用 →  UNITS 37C.38E and 39D

用may  或might    表示“可能”、“也许”.通常用may.    或用might,     可以说：
● It  may  be  true. 或 It  might  be  true.(可能是真的)
● She   might   know.  或 She   may   know.
否定式是may   not 和might    not (或mightn't):
● It  might  not  be  true. (可能不是真的)
● I'm  not   sure  whether   I   can  lend  you   any  money. I   may   not  have   enough.  (可能我也 不够)
过去式是may   have   (done)或  might    have(done):

有时 could 与may 和 might   的意思相同：
●   The   phone's    ringing.It   could   be    Tim.(=it   may/might   be    Tim)
●  You  could  have  left  your  bag  in  the   shop.(=you  may/might  have  left  it...) 但是couldn’t   (否定式)与may    not 和m ight    not 不同。试比较：

should (1)
You should do something =it is a good thing to do or the right thing to do should    用来提出建议、劝告：
● You look tired.You should go to bed.
● The government should do more to help homeless people.
●'Should we invite  Susan to the party?"‘Yes,I think we  should.'
I   think/I   don't   think/ Do  you  think...? 后面的从句中常用should:
● I think the government should do more to help homeless people.
● I don't think you should work so hard.
● 'Do you think I should apply for this job?    ‘Yes,I think you  should.' 'You shouldn't do something'=it isn't a good thing to do:
●   You   shouldn't   believe   everything   you   read   in   the   newspapers. should   没有must   语气那样肯定：
●  You  should   apologise.   ( 这是应该做的事)
● You   must   apologise.(你已经没有其他选择)
should    还用来表示某事不太对头或像我们想像的那样：
● I wonder where Liz is.She should be here by now. ( 她不在这儿，这很不正常)
● The price on this packet is wrong.It should be f1.20,not    f1.50.
● Those boys shouldn't be playing football at this time.They should be at school.
should    用于表示希望做或发生的事 ：
● She's been studying hard for the exam,so she should pass. (我希望她及格)
● There are plenty of hotels in the town.It shouldn't   be difficult to find somewhere to stay. (我不认为这 会有困难)
You should have done something =you didn't do it but it would have been the right thing to do:
● It  was  a  great  party  last  night. You  should   have    come. Why    didn't    you? ( 你没来，但 如果你来了会很高兴的)
●  I’m  feeling  sick.I   shouldn't   have   eaten    so    much     chocolate. (  我吃了太多的巧克力)
● I wonder why they're so late.They should have been here an hour ago.
●  She  shouldn't have been  listening to  our conversation.It was private. 请比较 should  (do)  与should  have  (done):
● You look tired.You should go to bed now.
● You went to bed very late last night.You should have gone to bed earlier.
ought  to …
在本页的句子中可以用ought   to代替should,    注意 ought  to  do. …  中 要 加 to:
● Do you think I ought to apply for this job?(=Do you think I should apply.…?)
● Jack ought not to go to bed so late.(=Jack shouldn’t go...)
● It was a great party last night.You ought to have come.
● She's been studying hard for the exam,so she ought to pass.

●  You  should   apologise.   ( 这是应该做的事)
● You   must   apologise.(你已经没有其他选择)
should    还用来表示某事不太对头或像我们想像的那样：
● I wonder where Liz is.She should be here by now. ( 她不在这儿，这很不正常)
● The price on this packet is wrong.It should be f1.20,not    f1.50.
● Those boys shouldn't be playing football at this time.They should be at school.
should    用于表示希望做或发生的事 ：
● She's been studying hard for the exam,so she should pass. (我希望她及格)
● There are plenty of hotels in the town.It shouldn't   be difficult to find somewhere to stay. (我不认为这 会有困难)
You should have done something =you didn't do it but it would have been the right thing to do:
● It  was  a  great  party  last  night. You  should   have    come. Why    didn't    you? ( 你没来，但 如果你来了会很高兴的)
●  I’m  feeling  sick.I   shouldn't   have   eaten    so    much     chocolate. (  我吃了太多的巧克力)
● I wonder why they're so late.They should have been here an hour ago.
●  She  shouldn't have been  listening to  our conversation.It was private. 请比较 should  (do)  与should  have  (done):
● You look tired.You should go to bed now.
● You went to bed very late last night.You should have gone to bed earlier.
ought  to …
在本页的句子中可以用ought   to代替should,    注意 ought  to  do. …  中 要 加 to:
● Do you think I ought to apply for this job?(=Do you think I should apply.…?)
● Jack ought not to go to bed so late.(=Jack shouldn’t go...)
● It was a great party last night.You ought to have come.
● She's been studying hard for the exam,so she ought to pass.
should  (2)→ UNIT 34        should 与  had better 比较→ LUNT 35B

had better       It's time...
had  better(I 'd better /you'd better   等)
I'd   better   do     something  表示“最好去做，如不做则会有困难或危险”:
● I have to meet Ann in ten minutes.I'd better go now or I'll be late.
●  Shall  I  take  an  umbrella?'‘Yes,you'd  better.It  might  rain.'
● We'd better stop for petrol soon.The tank is almost empty. 否定形式是I'd  better  not(=I  had  better  not):
● A:Are you going out tonight?
B:I'd better not.I've got a lot of work to do.
● You don't look very well.You'd better not go to work today. 用had       better  表示提醒或警告：
● You'd  better  be   on  time./ You'd better not be late. ( 否则 I'll be very angry) 请注意：
had  better  与 should  比较
had   better 与shoul d 意义相似(参见第33单元A 节),但并不完全一样
had   better   只用在表示某一特殊情况，而should  用在表示提出看法、劝告的所有情况中：
●  It's  cold  today.You'd  better  wear  a  coat  when  you  go  out. ( 特殊情况)
● I  think  all  drivers  should  wear  seat  belts.(一般情况——不用 had better wear)
另外，had     better 表示如不听从劝告总是会有危险或遇到困难的：而should    只意为it   is   a   good   thing   to
do . 试比较：
● It's   a   great    film.You  should  go  and  see  it.(但如不做既不会有困难，也不会有危险)
● The film starts at 8.30.You'd better go now or you'll be late.
It's  time..
可以说 It's time (for somebody) to  do  something:
● It's time to go home./It's time for us to go home.
还可以说：
● It's late.It's time we went home.
这句中过去时 (went)   用来表示现在或将来，而不是过去发生的动作：
●  It's   10   o'clock  and  he's   still  in  bed.It's  time  he  got  up. (而非 it's   time   he   gets  up)  It's  time  you  did  something      表示应该已经做了或开始做了：这种结构常用来表示批评或抱怨：
● It's time the children were in bed.It's long after their bedtime.
●  The  windows  are  very  dirty.I  think  it's  time  we  cleaned  them.
还可以说It's   about   time.../   It's    high    time      表示更为强烈的批评：
·Jack is a great talker.But it's about time he did something instead of just talking.
·You're    very    selfish.It's    high    time    you    realised    that    you're    not    the    most     important    person in   the   world.

Can/Could/Would you...? 等
(请求，给予帮助，允许及邀请)
请求做事
can   或could   用干表示请求某人做某事：
●   Can  you  wait   a   moment, please?   或 Could   you   wait    a   moment,please?
●  Liz,can  you  do  me  a  favour?
●  Excuse  me,could  you  tell  me  how  to  get to   the   station?
●  I  wonder  if  you  could  help  me.
注意可以说 Do     you     think(you)could.?  (一般不 用 can  ):
●  Do  you  think  you  could  lend  me  some money   until   next   week?
will  和 would   也用于表示请求(但 can/could    更常用):
●  Liz,will  you  do  me  a  favour?
·Would   you   please    be    quiet?I'′m    trying    to   concentrate.
请求给予
用Can   I   have...? 或 Could    I    have...? 表示请求给予：
● (在商店)Can I have these postcards,please?
● (在用餐过程中)Could  I  have  the  salt,please? May   I   have...?   也表达同样的意思(但不那么常用):
●   May   I   have    these   postcards,please?
请求允许
can,could      或 may  用于表示请求允许：
● (在电话中)Hello,can   I   speak   to   Tom, please?
●    'Could    I    use    your    phone?"‘Yes,of    course.'
●  Do  you  think  I  could  borrow  your  bike?
●    'May    I    come    in?''Yes,please    do.' can  或 may   用于表示允许：
● You  can   use   the   phone.       或 You   may  use  the  phone. may   比 ca n或 could   更正式，但不太常用
主动提出做某事
Can    I...?用于表示主动提出做某事：
·'Can   Iget   you    a    cup    of   coffee?"‘Yes,that    would    be   very    nice.'
●  'Can  I  help  you?”     No,it's    all    right.I    can    manage.' 还可以用I'll  表示主动去做某事(参见第21单元C 节):

给予和邀请
Would  you  like.…?   (而非 do   you   like ) 用于表示给予或邀请：
·'Would    you     like     a    cup    of    coffee?"‘Yes,please.'

UNIT
Can/Could/Would you...? 等
(请求，给予帮助，允许及邀请)
请求做事
can   或could   用干表示请求某人做某事：
●   Can  you  wait   a   moment, please?   或 Could   you   wait    a   moment,please?
●  Liz,can  you  do  me  a  favour?
●  Excuse  me,could  you  tell  me  how  to  get to   the   station?
●  I  wonder  if  you  could  help  me.
注意可以说 Do     you     think(you)could.?  (一般不 用 can  ):
●  Do  you  think  you  could  lend  me  some money   until   next   week?
will  和 would   也用于表示请求(但 can/could    更常用):
●  Liz,will  you  do  me  a  favour?
·Would   you   please    be    quiet?I'′m    trying    to   concentrate.
请求给予
用Can   I   have...? 或 Could    I    have...? 表示请求给予：
● (在商店)Can I have these postcards,please?
● (在用餐过程中)Could  I  have  the  salt,please? May   I   have...?   也表达同样的意思(但不那么常用):
●   May   I   have    these   postcards,please?
请求允许
can,could      或 may  用于表示请求允许：
● (在电话中)Hello,can   I   speak   to   Tom, please?
●    'Could    I    use    your    phone?"‘Yes,of    course.'
●  Do  you  think  I  could  borrow  your  bike?
●    'May    I    come    in?''Yes,please    do.' can  或 may   用于表示允许：
● You  can   use   the   phone.       或 You   may  use  the  phone. may   比 ca n或 could   更正式，但不太常用
主动提出做某事
Can    I...?用于表示主动提出做某事：
·'Can   Iget   you    a    cup    of   coffee?"‘Yes,that    would    be   very    nice.'
●  'Can  I  help  you?”     No,it's    all    right.I    can    manage.' 还可以用I'll  表示主动去做某事(参见第21单元C 节):
●  You  look  tired.I'l  get  you  a  cup  of  coffee.
给予和邀请
Would  you  like.…?   (而非 do   you   like ) 用于表示给予或邀请：
·'Would    you     like     a    cup    of    coffee?"‘Yes,please.'
·‘Would   you    like    to    come    to    dinner    tomorrow    evening?”‘Yes,Pd    love    to.' I'd     like... 是表达愿望的一种客气的说法：
● (问讯处)Pd   like   some    information   about   hotels,please.
● (在商店)Pd  like  to  try   on  this  jacket,please.

UNIT
37  If I do... 与 If I did... 比较
请看下列例句：
用if+  过去时(if I found/if    you     were  /if  we didn't   等)表示虚拟 的事情，但不是指过去的时间发生的事情：
·What  would  you  do  if  you  won  a  million  pounds? (我们并不认为会真实发生)
·I don't really want to go to their party,but I probably will go.They'd be offended if I didn't go.
·Sarah has decided not to apply for the job.She isn't really qualified for it,so she probably wouldn't get it if she applied.
if 从句中一般不用would:
IfI  won a million poundo …
·   I'd  be  very  frightened  if somebody  pointed  a  gun  at  me. (而非if   somebody   would   point)
●If  I   didn't  go  to  their  party,they'd  be  offended. (而非 If  I  wouldn't  go) if...  would     可以用来表示请求某人做某事：
●(正式信函中) I would be grateful if you  would  send  me  your  brochure  as  soon  as possible.
● 'Shall I close the door?"‘Yes,please,if you would.'
C       would( 'd)/wouldn't     用于句子的主句(不是 if 从句)中：
● If you took more exercise,you'd(=you would)probably feel healthier.
·Would you mind  if I used your phone?
● I’m  not  tired  enough  to  go  to  bed  yet.I  wouldn't  sleep.(如果我现在上床) could   和 might   也可以使用：
● If you took more exercise,you  might feel healthier. (很可能你会感到身体健康一些)
● If  it  stopped  raining,we  could  go  out. (我们会外出)
下列句子中不能用when:
● They  would  be   offended  if  we   didn't   accept     their    invitation.( 而非when    we     didn't)
●  What   would   you   do   if  you   were   bitten   by   a   snake?(而非when    you    were     bitten) if 和when   的用法另参见第25单元C 节。

41  被动语态(1)(is done /was done)
请看下列例句：
This house was built in 1930.
was built是被动语态，比较主动语态与被动语态：
Somebody built this house in 1930. (主动)
主语           宾语
This house was built in 1930. (被动)
主语
主动语态用于说明动作的执行者：
● My grandfather was a builder.He built this house in 1930.
● It's a big company.It employs two hundred people.
被动语态用于说明动作的承受者：
● This house is quite old.It was built in 1930.
● Two hundred people are employed by the company.
使用被动语态时往往不知道或没有必要知道动作的执行者：
● A  lot  of  money was stolen   in  the  robbery. (有人偷但不知道是谁)
●  Is  this  room  cleaned  every  day?(谁清扫的?——知道是谁做的不重要) 如果需要说明动作的执行者时，可以用介词 by..:
● This house was built by my grandfather.
● Two hundred people are employed by the company.
被动语态由be  (is/was/have  been)+ 过去分词(done/cleaned/seen )构成：
(be)done         (be)cleaned         (be) seen        (be)damaged          (be)built     等
不规则动词的过去分词(done/known/seen)见附录1
请看一般现在时和一般过去时的被动语态形式：
被动语态(其他形式) → UNITS  42-43       by→ UHIT 127C

间接引语(1)(He said that...)
请看下列例句：
间接引语通常由过去时的动词(主要动词)引入，直接引语中的动词通常也变成过去时态：
● Tom said that he was feeling ill.
●  I  told  her  that  I  didn't  have  any  money.
这时，that 可以省略：
● Tom said(that)he was feeling ill.            ● I told her (that)I didn't have any money. 一般情况下，直接引语中的动词现在时的形式变成过去时形式：
am/is → was               do/does→did                will →would
are →were                 have/has →had               can →→could
want/like/knowgo 等  → wanted/liked/knew/went 等 请比较下面的直接引语与间接引语：
直接引语中的一般过去时(did/saw/knew 等)在间接引语中通常可以保持不变，或者也可以变成过去完成 时(had     done/had     seen/had     known 等):
直接   Tom said:'I woke up feeling ill,so I didn't go to work.'
间接   Tom   said(that)he woke up feelingill,so he didn't go to work.     或
Tom said(that)he had woken up feling ill so he hadn't gone to work.

1 George's nose was broken in a fight.
What  happened  to  George? …He had his nose broken in a fight. …
2  Sarah's bag was  stolen  on  a train.
What happened to Sarah?She ……………………………………………………………………………………………………
3 Fred's hat was blown off in the wind.
What happened to Fred? … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … …             ………
4 Diane's passport was taken away from her by the police.
What happened to Diane? … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … …       …
间接引语(1)(He said that...)
请看下列例句：
间接引语通常由过去时的动词(主要动词)引入，直接引语中的动词通常也变成过去时态：
● Tom said that he was feeling ill.
●  I  told  her  that  I  didn't  have  any  money.
这时，that 可以省略：
● Tom said(that)he was feeling ill.            ● I told her (that)I didn't have any money. 一般情况下，直接引语中的动词现在时的形式变成过去时形式：
am/is → was               do/does→did                will →would
are →were                 have/has →had               can →→could
want/like/knowgo 等  → wanted/liked/knew/went 等 请比较下面的直接引语与间接引语：
直接引语中的一般过去时(did/saw/knew 等)在间接引语中通常可以保持不变，或者也可以变成过去完成 时(had     done/had     seen/had     known 等):
直接   Tom said:'I woke up feeling ill,so I didn't go to work.'
间接   Tom   said(that)he woke up feelingill,so he didn't go to work.     或
Tom said(that)he had woken up feling ill so he hadn't gone to work.
间接引语(2)→ UNIT 47      间接疑问句 → DWI 49B
UNIT

间接引语(2)
在间接引语中，并不是都要改变动词的时态  如果转述的事实仍然成立或有效，就不用改变动词 的时态了：
直接    Tom said 'New York is more lively than London.'
间接    Tom said that New York is more lively than London.
(纽约仍充满活力，只是说话的情景变了)
直接     Ann  said  'I  want  to  go  to  New  York  next  year.'
间接     Ann  said  that  she  wants  to  go  to  New  York  next  year.
(安仍想明年去纽约)
注意在以上情况下，将动词改成过去时也是正确的：
● Tom said that New York was more lively than London.
● Ann said that she  wanted to go to New York next year.
但如果转述的与事实不相符合时， 一定要用过去时：
say与tell 比较
如果要讲明说话对象，用tell:
●Sonia  told  me  that  you  were  ill. (而非Sonia   said   me)           TELL SOMEBODY
·   What did you tellthe police?(而非say the  police) 否则用say:
·Sonia  said  that  you  were  ill. (而非Sonia   told    that…)
●What did you say?                                                                                     s2r 但是可以说 say     something   to    somebody:
● Ann  said  goodbye  to  me  and  left.(而非 Ann   said   me  goodbye)
·What  did  you  say  to  the  police?
tell/ask somebody to do something
在间接引语中，特别是在tell   和 ask( 表示命令和请示)之后，还可以使用动词的不定式形式：
● 直接   'Stay in bed for a few days, the doctor said to me. 间接    The doctor told me to stay in bed for a few days.
●  直接    'Don't shout,'I  said to Jim.
间接    I told Jim not to shout.
· 直接    'Please don't tell anybody what happened,'Ann said to me.
间接    Ann asked me not to tell anybody what (had)happened.
也可用...said  to  do  something:
·T   he  doctor  said  to  stay  in  bed  for  a  few  days.(而非The    doctor   said    me..)

49.3      你外出了一段时间，刚回到家乡、你见到你的朋友格里，他问你下列问题：
用间接引语告诉另一位朋友格里问你的问题
1  … HeaskedmehowIwas …
2 He asked me   ………………………………………………………………………………………
3 He  …………………………………………………………………………………………………  4    ……………………………………………………………………………………………………… 5    ……………………………………………………………………………………………………… 6   ……………………………………………………………………………………………………… 7     ……………………………………………………………………………………………………… 8     ……………………………………………………………………………………………………… 9     ………………………………………………………………………………………………………
助动词(have/do/can 等)
I think so/I hope so 等
下面的句子都用了两个动词：
这些句子中的 have/can't/  was/do    是助动词
为了避免重复，在回答问题或写后半句话时可以只用助动词：
●  'Have  you  locked  the   door?"‘Yes,I  have.'(=Ihave locked the door)
●  George  wasn't  working  but  Janet  was.(=Janet  was  working)
● She could lend me the money but she won't.(=she won't  lend me  the money)
●'Are    you    angry    with    me?'‘Of   course    I'm    not.’(=I’m   not    angry) do/ does/did   用于一般现在时和一般过去时：
●  'Do  you  like  onions?"‘Yes,I  do.'(=I  like  onions)
● 'Does Mark smoke?"'He did but he doesn't any more.'
用have        you?/isn't        she?/do         they?  等形式有礼貌地表示对问题感兴趣：
●     I've    just     met     Simon.'‘Oh,have     you?How     is     he?"
●  'Liz  isn't  very  well  today.'‘Oh,isn't  she?What's  wrong  with  her?"
●   'It  rained   every   day   during   our   holiday.       'Did   it?What   a    pity!" 有时用“简略问句”表示惊讶：
●  'Jim  and  Nora   are  getting  married.'‘Are  they?Really?"
用so  和 neither    结构时，要用助动词：
●'I’m  feeling  tired.'‘So  am  I.'(=I’m  feeling  tired  too)
●'I never read newspapers.'    'Neither do I.'(=I never read newspapers either)
● Sue hasn't got a car and neither has Martin.
注意SO 和neither    之后的词序(动词置于主语前):
● I passed  the  exam  and  so  did  Tom.(而非so  Tom    did) 可以用nor   代替 neither:
●  'I  can't  remember  his  name.'  'Nor  can  I.'  或'Neither   can   I.' 还可以用…not …either:
● 'I  haven't  got   any  money.'  'Neither  have   I.'  或'Nor  have  I.'  或 Ihaven't        either.'
I    think    so/Ihope  so 等
为了避免重复、在有些动词之后可以用SO:
●  'Are  those  people  English?’I  think  so.'(=I  think they are English)
●  ‘Will  you  be  at  home  tomorrow  morning?''I  expect   so.'(= I expect I'll be  at home...)
● 'Do you think Kate has been invited to the party?'I suppose so.' 还可以说I hope so,I guess so 和I'm  afraid so。
通常的否定形式是：
● 'Is that woman American?"I think so./I don't think so.'
●  'Do  you  think  it's  going  to  rain?’'I     hope     so./Ihope     not.'(而非I   don't  hope   so)
EXERCISES

●'Are    you    angry    with    me?'‘Of   course    I'm    not.’(=I’m   not    angry) do/ does/did   用于一般现在时和一般过去时：
●  'Do  you  like  onions?"‘Yes,I  do.'(=I  like  onions)
● 'Does Mark smoke?"'He did but he doesn't any more.'
用have        you?/isn't        she?/do         they?  等形式有礼貌地表示对问题感兴趣：
●     I've    just     met     Simon.'‘Oh,have     you?How     is     he?"
●  'Liz  isn't  very  well  today.'‘Oh,isn't  she?What's  wrong  with  her?"
●   'It  rained   every   day   during   our   holiday.       'Did   it?What   a    pity!" 有时用“简略问句”表示惊讶：
●  'Jim  and  Nora   are  getting  married.'‘Are  they?Really?"
用so  和 neither    结构时，要用助动词：
●'I’m  feeling  tired.'‘So  am  I.'(=I’m  feeling  tired  too)
●'I never read newspapers.'    'Neither do I.'(=I never read newspapers either)
● Sue hasn't got a car and neither has Martin.
注意SO 和neither    之后的词序(动词置于主语前):
● I passed  the  exam  and  so  did  Tom.(而非so  Tom    did) 可以用nor   代替 neither:
●  'I  can't  remember  his  name.'  'Nor  can  I.'  或'Neither   can   I.' 还可以用…not …either:
● 'I  haven't  got   any  money.'  'Neither  have   I.'  或'Nor  have  I.'  或 Ihaven't        either.'
I    think    so/Ihope  so 等
为了避免重复、在有些动词之后可以用SO:
●  'Are  those  people  English?’I  think  so.'(=I  think they are English)
●  ‘Will  you  be  at  home  tomorrow  morning?''I  expect   so.'(= I expect I'll be  at home...)
● 'Do you think Kate has been invited to the party?'I suppose so.' 还可以说I hope so,I guess so 和I'm  afraid so。
通常的否定形式是：
● 'Is that woman American?"I think so./I don't think so.'
●  'Do  you  think  it's  going  to  rain?’'I     hope     so./Ihope     not.'(而非I   don't  hope   so)

have     you?和wasn't    it? 是附加疑问，即口语中附加在句子后面的简略问话。 do/does/did     用于一般现在 时和一般过去时的附加疑问中，在其他各时态之后则用助动词(如have/was/wil   l等),
●  'Karen  plays  the  piano,doesn't  she?'‘Well,yes,but  not  very  well.'
●  'You  didn't  lock  the  door,did  you?'      'No,I    forgot.'
在肯定句后，我们一般用否定的附加疑问：
同样，在否定句之后，用肯定的附加疑问：
注意：对否定疑问句作出回答时 Yes 和No  的含义：
●  You're  not  going  out  today,are  you?
附加疑问的意思取决于说话人的语调、如果用降调，说话人通常不需要对方提供情况，而是希望得到对 方的认可：
●    'It's    a    nice    day,isn't    it?‘Yes,lovely.'
●  'Tim  doesn't  look  well  today,does he?'  'No,he  looks  very  tired.'
●  She's  very  pretty.She's  got  beautiful  eyes,hasn't  she?
如果说话人用升调，则是提出一个希望得到回答的真正的问句：
●'You haven't  seen  Mary  today,have you?"'No,I'm    afraid    not.'
(=Have you seen Mary today by any chance?)
我们常常用否定句+肯定附加疑问的形式来询问有关情况，或让某人干某事。这时附加疑问句用升调： ●'You  haven't  got  a  pen,have ygu?'     ‘Yes,here  you   are.'
●  'You  couldn't  do  me  a  favour,could  you?"'It  depends  what  it  is.'
●‘You  don't  know  where  Karen  is, do you?”'Sorry,I've     no     idea.'
let's   后的附加疑问用...shall    we?
● Let's go for a walk,shall we?
在祈使句( Do.../Don't       do.. 等),附加疑问通常用...will    you?
·Open   the   door,will   you?                            ●  Don't  be  late,will  you?
●  I’m  late,aren't  I?

动词+-ing形式(enjoy doing/stop doing 等)
请看下列例句：
● I enjoy   dancing.(而非 I  enjoy  to  dance)
● Would   you   mind   closing   the   door? (  而非mind to close)
● lan   suggested   going to the    cinema. (  而非suggested    to    go) 在enjoy, mind  和 suggest   之后的动词要用-ing  形 式
在下表中的动词之后均跟动词的-ing 形 式
● Suddenly everybody stopped talking.There was silence.
● I'lldo the shopping when I've finished cleaning the flat.
● He tried to avoid answering my question.
● I don't fancy going out this evening.
●   Have    you   ever    considered   going    to   live    in    another   country? 注意：否定形式是not     -ing:
● When I'm on holiday,I enjoy not having to get up early.
在一些短语后面也用动词的-ing 形式：
● Paula has given up smoking.
●W e must do something.We can't go on living like this!(或 . .carry on living...)
●     Don't  keep  interrupting  me  while  I’m   speaking.    (或 Don't   keep      on     interrupting..)
C    在有些动词后可以用动词+宾语+-ing 结构：
● I can't imagine George riding a motorbike.
● You can't stop me doing what I want.
● 'Sorry to keep you waiting so long.'‘That's all right.' 注意： -ing 的被动形式 ( being    done/seen/kept     等):
●  I  don't  mind  being  kept  waiting.(=I  don't  mind  people  keeping  me..)
谈论已完成的行为时，可以用having    done/stolen/said  等：
● She admitted having stolen the money.
除 了having(done),      也可以用-ing 的一般式来表达已经完成的行为。如：
● She admitted stealing the money.
●  I  now  regret   saying  (or  having   said)what  I   said.
regret  的用法参见第55单元B 节。
上述的一些动词(尤其是admit/deny/suggest   ) 之后，可以跟that 从句。
● She denied that she had stolen the money.( 或 She denied stealing..)
● Ian  suggested  that  we  went  to  the  cinema. ( 或Ia n   suggested   going..) suggest  的用法参见第34单元。
动词+ to. …→ UN153         动 词 +t...      与 -Ing  形式比较 →LIM1S 54C.5557             regret/go on→ INI15B

● I like playing/to play tennis.(=Ienjoy it in general)
● I would like to play tennis today.(=I want to play today) 注意：would   mind后面跟动词的-ing 形式：
● Would you mind closing the door,please?
可以说 I would like to have done  something(  = I regret now that I didn't or couldn't do something):
● It's a pity we didn't see Val when we were in London.I would like to have seen her again.
● We'd like to have gone on holiday but we didn't have enough money. would love/would hate/ would prefer 之后也可以用同样的结构：
● Poor old Tom!I would hate to have been in his position.
● I'd love to have gone to the party but it was impossible.

057.title: 动词(like / would   like等)+-ing  形式或to..(3)
057.explanation:
1  a   I was very tired.I tried.…to keep  (keep)my eyes open but I couldn’t.
b I rang the doorbellbut there was no answer.Then I tried … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … (knock)on the door,but there was still no answer.
c We tried ……………………………………………(put)the fire out but we were unsuccessful.We had to call the fire brigade.
d Sue needed to borrow some money.She tried … … … … … … … … … … …      ……(ask)Gerry but he was short of money too.
e I tried……………………………………………(reach)the shelf but I wasn't tall enough.
f Please leave me alone.I'm trying … … … … … … … … …     …   ………(concentrate).
2 a I need a change.I need…………………………………………(go)away for a while.
b She isn't able to look after herself.She needs ……………………………………(look)after.
c The windows are dirty.They need……………………………………………(clean).
d Why are you leaving now.You don't need … … … … … … … … … …     ……   …(go)yet,do   you? e You don't need … … … … … … … … … … … … … … …     ………(iron)that  shirt.It  doesn't  need
…………………………………………(iron).
3  a They were talking very loudly.I couldn't help … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … (overhear)them. b Can you help me ………………………………………(get)the dinner ready?
c  He  looks  so  funny.Whenever  I  see  him,I  can't  help … … … …         ………………(smile).
d The fine weather helped … … …       …………………………… (make)it a very enjoyable holiday.
上述动词和短语都是“喜欢” 或“不喜欢”的意思，后面常跟动词的-ing形式：
●   Ann   hates   flying.
● Why  do  you  dislike  living  here?
● I don't like people shouting at me.(=I don't like being shouted at.) 在love, hate 和 can't  bear  之后也可以用动词不定式：
● Ilove   meeting   people.   或   I  ove  to  meet   people.
● She  can't  bear  being  alone.     或   She  can't  bear  to  be  alone. 但在enjoy     /dislike/mind     /can't     stand,之后只能用动词的-ing 形式：
● I enjoy being alone.  ( 而非Ienjoy   to   be)
●  Tom  doesn't  mind  working at   night. (而非mind   to   work)
like
可以说 I like doing  something    或 I  like  to  do  something: 用哪一种都可以：
●  I  like  getting   up    early.     或      I  like  to  get   up  early.
但在英国英语中，I like   doing   和 I  like  to do  有时有区别
I  like  doing something     意思是“我乐意这样做”
● Do you like cooking?(=do you enjoy it?)
● I like living here.(=Ienjoy it)
I  like  to  do   something  意为“我认为这样做妥当”:
● I like to clean the kitchen as often as possible.(This doesn't mean that I enjoy it;it means that I think it is a good thing to do.)
● Mary likes people to be on time.
would      like/would      love/would      hate/would      prefer 后面通常跟动词不定式：
● I would like to be rich.
● Would you like to come to dinner on Friday?
● I'dlove(=would love)to be able to travel round the world.
● Would you prefer to have dinner now or later?
请比较 I  like  与 I would   like:
● I like playing/to play tennis.(=Ienjoy it in general)
● I would like to play tennis today.(=I want to play today) 注意：would   mind后面跟动词的-ing 形式：
● Would you mind closing the door,please?
可以说 I would like to have done  something(  = I regret now that I didn't or couldn't do something):
● It's a pity we didn't see Val when we were in London.I would like to have seen her again.
● We'd like to have gone on holiday but we didn't have enough money. would love/would hate/ would prefer 之后也可以用同样的结构：
● Poor old Tom!I would hate to have been in his position.
● I'd love to have gone to the party but it was impossible.
would like →

prefer   与 would rather 比较
prefer to do 与prefer doing
可以用 prefer  to  (do)  或 prefer  -ing形式来表示优先选择：
● I don't like cities. I prefer to live in the country.       或  I prefer living in the  country.
I prefer this coat to the coat you were wearing yesterday.
● I prefer driving to travelling by train.
但是说● I prefer to drive rather than travel by train.
● Ann prefers to live in the country rather than (live)in a city.
would prefer (I'd prefer..)
可以用 would    prefer 来表示“宁愿”:
●  'Would  you  prefer  tea  or  coffee?”'Coffee,please.' 可以说would prefer to do (而非doing):
●   'Shall   we    go   by    train?'‘Well,I'd prefer to go  by  car.'( 而非I'd prefer   going)
● I'd prefer to stay at home tonight rather than go to the cinema.
would  rather(I'd  rather..)
would       rather (do)=     would      prefer  (to   do)。would   rather    之后可以跟不带to  的动词不定式。 请比较：
●'Shall we go by train?'
●  'Would  you  rather  have  tea  or  coffee?’‘Coffee,please. 否定式是 I'd rather not (do  something):
● I’m tired.I'd rather not go out this evening,if you don't mind.
● 'Do you want to go out this evening?'T'd rather not.'
请看下面would rather之后的结构：
● I'd rather stay at home tonight than go to the cinema.
I'd rather you did something
想要某人干某事，可以说 I'd  rather  you  did  something:
●‘Shall    I    stay    here?”Td    rather    you     came    with    us.'
●    'Shall    I    tellthem    the    news?.'No.Id    rather    they    didn't    know.'
●  Shall  I  tell  them  or  would  you  rather  they  didn't  know? 这时要用过去时态(came, did 等),但表示的意思是现在或将来。
请比较：
● I'd rather cook the dinner now.
但是 · I'd rather you cooked the dinner now.(而非I'd rather you cook) 否定形式是 I'd rather you didn't  ….:
● I'd rather you didn't tellanyone what I said.
● 'Do you mind if I smoke?"    T'd rather you didn't.'
prefer..o..→     UNIT 1350

would prefer (I'd prefer..)

would  rather(I'd  rather..)

63 to….,for.. 和so that... 比较(表目的)
A     通常用不定式表示目的：
●'Why  did  you  go  out?''To  post  a  letter.'
● A friend of mine phoned to invite me to a party.
●We   shouted   to   warn   everybody   of   the   danger.
不定式还可以表示原因：
● This wall is to keep people out of the garden.
● The President has a team of bodyguards to protect him.
● I need a bottle opener to open this bottle.
B      不定式还可以放在名词或代词后表示它们可以怎么使用或处置：
● It's difficult to find a place to park in the city centre.(=a place where you can park)
● Would you like something to eat?
● Have you got much work to do?(=work that you must do)
● I get lonely if there's nobody to talk to.
此外：money/time/chance/opportunity/energy/         courage   等动词后面也可以跟to  (do  something):
●  They  gave  us  some  money  to  buy  some  food.
● Do you have much opportunity to practise your English?
● I need a few days to think about your proposal.
C    for … 和to … 比较 请比较：
● I'm going to Spain for a holiday.
但说：   I'm    going    to     Spain    to    learn     Spanish. ( 而 非for   learn    Spanish,也不说 for   learning    Spanish)
for …  之后用名词， to …    之后用动词。再看些例子：
● What would you like for dinner?
而说： What would you like to eat?(而非for   eat)
● Let's go to the pool for a swim.
而说： Let's go to the pool to have a swim.
注意：可以说... for      (somebody)to(do      something):
● There weren't any chairs for us to sit on,so we had to sit on the floor. for -ing 和to … 都可用来表示一般目的：
● This knife is only for cutting bread.(或 ...  to  cut  bread.) What….for? 用在疑问句中表示目的：
● What is this switch for?                             ● What did you do that for?
D     so  that
有时必须用so   that 来表示目的，so   that  后面不能跟动词不定式：

UNIT
67  -ing 短语(例如：Feeling tired,I went to bed early.)
短语是句子的一部分。有些句子有两个或更多的短语：
●    Jim    hurt    his    arm    playing    tennis.
主句
● Feeling tired,I went to bed early.
L- ing从句 JL            主句
playing   tennis     和 feeling    tired  是-ing  短语。
如果-ing  短语放在句首(如第二个例句),要用逗号与主句隔开。
同一主语的两个行为同时发生时，其中一个行为常可用-ing  形式来表示。这个短语一般放在主句的后面。
● I've just seen Carol.She's in the bar having a drink.(=she is in the bar and she is having a drink)
● A man ran out of the house shouting.(= he ran out ofthe house and he was shouting)
● Do something!Don't just stand there doing nothing!
如果一个行为发生时，另一个行为正在进行，另一个行为可以用-ing 短语来表示：
● Jim hurt his arm playing tennis.(=while he was playing)
● Did you cut yourself shaving?(=while you were shaving) 在while  或 when  后面也可以用-ing 结构：
● Jim hurt his arm while playing tennis.
● Be careful when crossing the road.(=when you are crossing)
如果一个行为发生在另一个行为之前，发生在先的行为用having  (done)  表示：
● Having found a hotel,we looked for somewhere to have dinner.
●  Having  finished  her  work,she  went  home.
after 之后也可用-ing 结构：
● After finishing her work,she went home.
如果一个短暂行为紧接着另一个短暂行为，第一个行为用-ing  的一般形式，而不用完成式(having      done):
● Taking a key out of his pocket,he opened the door.
这些结构在书面语中更常见。
-ing  短语还可以用来解释某事或说明原因，这时它常放在句首：
● Feeling tired,I went to bed early.(=because I felt tired)
● Being unemployed,he hasn't got much money.(=because he is unemployed)
● Not having a car,she finds it difficult to get around.(=because she doesn't have a car)
● Having already seen the film twice,I didn't want to go to the cinema.(=because I had already seen it twice)
这些结构在书面语中更常见。

UNIT
69  可数名词与不可数名词(2)
很多名词可以是可数名词也可以是不可数名词，但其意义不同：
coffee/tea/beer/juice     等(饮料)通常为不可数名词：
● I don't drink coffee very often.
然而，表示a   cup/a  glass 等的意思时，这些词可以用作可数名词。
● (在餐馆)Two  coffees  and  an  orange  juice,please.
有些名词在英语中是不可数的，但在其他语言中常常是可数的：
一般说来，这些名词是不可数的，所以：
i) 之前不能加 a/an (不能说 a     bread,  an   advice   等)
ii) 通常没有复数形式(不说 breads  , advices    等):
●  I'm  going  to  buy   some   bread.      或   .   ..a  loaf  of bread. ( 而非a   bread)
●Enjoy  your  holiday!I  hope  you  have  good   weather.(而非a good     weather)
●Where  are  you  going  to  put  al lyour      furniture?(而非furnitures) news   是不可数名词，不能把它看作名词的复数：
●  The  news  was  very depressing.   ( 而非the news were)
trave l  ( 名词)指笼统意义的旅游，不能用 a travel  来表示一次旅行：
●We  had  a  very  good  journey. (而非a   good  travel) 请比较下列可数与不可数名词：
可数名词与不可数名词(1)→ UNIT 68

可数名词的单复数
可数名词有单、复数之分：
a dog      a child                  the evening         this party           an umbrella
the evenings      these  parties       two umbrellas
单数可数名词前面要加 a/an:
Goodbye!Have a nice evening.
● Do you need an umbrella?
单数可数名词不能独立使用，必须有a/the/my   等：
● She   never   wears   a   hat. ( 而非She    never   wears   hat)
● Be   careful   of   the   dog. (而非Be   careful   of   dog)
● What a beautiful day!
● I've got a headache.
a/an    用来表示某物或某人属于哪一类别：
● A dog is an animal.
●  I'm  an  optimist.
● Tim's father is a doctor.
● Are you a good driver?
● Jill is a really nice person.
● What a lovely dress!
也可以说 somebody   has   a   long   nose/a   nice    face/ a   strong   heart 等：
Jack  has  got  a  long   nose. (而非the    long   nose) 复数名词可以独立使用，而不必与some   连用：
● Dogs are animals.
●Most of my friends are students.
●    Jill's    parents    are    really    nice    people.
●     What     awful     shoes!
● Jack  has  got  blue  eyes.(而非the blue eyes) 表示职业的名词前面要加 a/an:
● Sandra  is  a  nurse.(而非Sandra    is    nurse)
● Would you like to be an English teacher?
some    可以和复数可数名词连用，有两种表达法：
i)some=a  number  of/a  few  of  l  a  pair  of:
● I've  seen  some  good  films  recently.(而非I' ve  seen good films)
● Some friends of mine are coming to stay at the weekend.
●  I  need   some  new   sunglasses.(=a   new  pair   of  sunglasses) 表达全体的含义时不能用some  ( 参见第74单元):
● I  love bananas.  (而非some     bananas)
●My   aunt   is   a   writer.She   writes books.(而非some      books) 有时句中复数名词前面的 some  可以省略，意义不变：
● There are (some)eggs in the fridge if you're hungry. ii)some  =some  but not all
● Some children learn very quickly.(but not allchildren)
● Some police officers in Britain carry guns,but most of them don't.

在谈到彼此都知其所指的东西时，也用the 。例如：我们在房间里谈论 the    light    /the    floor   /the    ceiling/ the   door/the    carpet    等：

●I must go to the bank to get some money and then I'm going to the post office to get some stamps.(The speaker is usually thinking of a particular bank or post office.)
此外还可以说the  doctor,the  dentist:
● Carol isn't very well.She's gone to the doctor.(=her usual doctor)
● I hate going to the dentist.
请比较a:
● Is there a bank near here?
●My  sister  is  a  dentist.
在下列例句中，必须用the:
● Susan  works  in  the  city  centre.( 而非in   city   centre)
● My  brother  is  in  the  army.( 而非in   army)

the +形容词)

● The giraffe is the tallest of all animals.
● The bicycle is an excellent means of transport.

这些例句中的the  不是指某个具体的东西  The    giraffe   =one   particular   type    of   animal,not   one
particular     giraffe. the(+     单数可数名词)用来指整个类属 同样地，乐器前面也用the:

the   可以和形容词连用，代表一类人。例如：
the  young  the  old    the poor
the sick         the disabled
the elderly
the unemployed the injured
the homeless the dead
the young =young people,the rich =rich people 等：

● The homeless need more help from the government.
这样的表达法有复数的含义，不能说 a   young  或 an        unemployed 但可以说 a young  man, an    unemployed woman  等
注意：the poor (而非 the  poors),the  young (而非the     youngs)等。

the Spanish      the Dutch      the British      the Irish      the Welsh
注意：the   French/the   English  等含义是复数. 不能说 a    French   /an    English, 但可以说 a  Frenchman   / an   Englishwoman  等
还可以在表示国籍的词后面加-es e与the 连用，如the     Chinese/the      Sudanese等：

这些词也可以是单数( a    Japanese,a     Sudanese)。

UNIT
78 单数与复数
有时，名词以复数形式出现，表示由两部分构成的东西：
这些名词是复数，后面跟复数动词：
●  My  trousers  are  too   long. ( 而非is   too    long) 也可以用a  pair  of + 名词的复数：
● Those are nice jeans.     或  That's  a  nice pair  of jeans. (而非a  nice jeans)
●  I need  some  new  glasses.  或   I need  a  new  pair  of glasses.
一些以-ics 结尾的名词并不都是复数，例如：athletics   gymnastics  mathematics ( 或maths)physics electronics   economics   politics
●   Gymnastics   is   my   favourite   sport.
news   不是复数(参见第69单元C 节):
● What  time  is  the  news  on     television?( 而非are   the   news)
以-s  结尾的一些词可以是单数，也可以是复数：
means         a means  of transport     many means of transport
series         a television series          two television series
species         a species of bird             200 species of bird
一些单数名词常常和复数动词连用：
government      staff       team       family       audience       committee       company       firm
这些名词指组成整体的若干个人(they),    而不是某一个( it),    所以后面要跟复数动词：
●  The  government(=they)want  to  increase  taxes.
●  The  staff  at  the   school(=they)are  not  happy  with  their  new  working  conditions. 同样，运动队或公司的名称后面常常用复数动词：
● Scotland are playing France next week (in a footballmatch).
● Shell have increased the price of petrol.
这些名词也可以与单数动词连用(The     government     wants../Shell      has...等)。
police    后总是用复数动词：
● The  police have  arrested  a  friend  of mine. (而非The  police  has)
● Do   you  think  the  police  are  well-paid?
注意：指某一个警察要用 a    policeman/a     policewoman/a    police     officer  ( 而非a  police)。
person   一词不常有复数形式( persons),      一般用people   (表示复数的单词):
●  He's  a  nice   person.  但要说 They  are  nice  people.
● Many   people   don't   have   enough   to   eat.(  而非doesn't      have)
一笔钱、 一段时间、 一段距离等所指是一个整体数量时，后面跟单数动词：
●   Twenty   thousand   pounds  (= it)was stolen in the robbery.( 而非were stolen)
●   Three   years(= it)is  a  long  time  to  be  without  a  job.(而非Three   years    are...)
● Six miles is a long way to walk every day.

81  a friend of mine my own house on my own/by myself
a friend of mine/a friend of Tom's 等
通常说 a friend    of   mine/yours/his/hers/ours/theirs    (而非a friend of me/you/him等):
● I’m going to a wedding on Saturday.A  friend  of mine is getting married. (而非 a friend of me)
● We went on holiday with some friends of ours. (而非 some  friends of us)
● Michael had an argument with a neighbour of his.
● It was a good idea of yours to go swimming this afternoon.
同样地，a friend of Tom's,a friend of my sister's 等：
● It was a good idea of Tom's to go swimming.
● That woman over there is a friend of my sister's.
my  own.../   your   own… 等
own  的前面要用my/your/his/her/its/our/their:
my own house      your  own  car      her  own  room
不能说 an     own.  (an   own   house,  an  own  car等)。
my  own.…. / your   own. …  ( 等 ) = something   that    is    only   mine/yours( 等),not   shared   or
borrowed:
● I don't want to share a room with anybody.I want my own room.
●  Vera  and  George  would  like  to  have  their   own  house.(而非an   own   house)
● It's a pity that the flat hasn't got its own entrance.
● It's my own fault that I've got no money.I buy too many things I don't need.
● Why do you want to borrow my car?Why can't you use your own?(=your own car) …own …   也可以用来表示你自己，而不是他人替你干某事：
●  Brian  usually  cuts  his  own  hair.
(=he  cuts  it  himself;he  doesn't  go  to
the   hairdresser)
● I'd like to have a garden so that I could grow my own vegetables.
(= grow them myself instead of
buying them from shops)
on  my  own/by  myself
on  my  own 和by  myself 都是“独自”的意思。可以说：
● I like living on my own/by myself

UNIT
82  myself/yourself/themselves 等
请看下列例句：
反身代词有：
单数：      myself         yourself (一个人)              himself/herself/itself
复数：     ourselves      yourselves    (  两个以上的人)          themselves
● I  don't  want  you  to  pay  for  me.I'llpay  for  myself. (而非I'll  pay   for   me)
● Julia had a great holiday.She enjoyed herself very much.
● Do  you  sometimes  talk  to  yourself? ( 只对一个人讲)
● If you  want  more  to  eat,help  yourselves. (对两个以上的人讲) 请比较：
● It's not our fault.You can't blame us.
●  It's  our  own  fault.We  blame  ourselves.
注意 bring/take     something    with. 后面不用反身代词。
●  It  might  rain.I'ltake    an   umbrella   with   me. (而非with    myself)
concentrate/   feel /relax/meet 后面不用反身代词：
● You   must   try   and   concentrate. (而非concentrate        yourself)
●  'Do  you  feel  nervous?'‘Yes,I  can't  relax.'
● What  time   shall  we  meet?(而非meet ourselves,  而非meet    us) wash/shave/dress    后通常没有反身代词：
●  He   got  up, washed,  shaved   and   dressed.(而非washed   himself  等) 但是说 I dried  myself.
区分-selves 与each   other:
● Tom and Ann stood in front of the mirror and looked at themselves.(=T om and Ann looked at Tom  and Ann)
但要说 Tom looked at Ann;Ann looked at Tom.They looked at each other.
one   another 与each   other 可以互换：
● How long have you and Bill known one  another?( 或说...known  each  other)
●  Sue  and  Ann  don't  like   each   other.(或说..don'  t  like  one  another)
反身代词还有另一种用法：
● ‘Who repaired your bicycle for you?"'Nobody.I repaired it myself.'
I  repaired  it  myself  =I   repaired   it, not  anybody     else. 这时，myself 用来强调 I( =it   makes   it stronger) 。 再看几个例句：
● I'm  not  going  to  do  it  for  you.You  can  do  it  yourself. (= you,   而非 me)
● Let's paint the house ourselves.It will be much cheaper.
● The film itself wasn't very good but I liked the music.
●  I  don't  think   Sue  willget  the  job. Sue herself  doesn't  think  she'll  get  it. (或 Sue doesn't think she'll get it herself.)
get(dressed/marrled等)→ UK1143     by myeelf/by yourself 等→ UMIT 81C

第一次谈论某件事时，用there …   表明人或物等的存在：
● There's  a  new  restaurant  in  King   Street. (而非A  new  restaurant  is  in  King   Street)
●  The  journey  took  a  long  time. There was a lot   of  traffic.( 而非It  was  a  lot  of  traffic)
● Things are much more expensive now.There has been a big rise in the cost of living. it=a   particular   thing,place,fact,situation   etc.(参见C 节):
●We  went  to  the  new  restaurant.It's  very  good.(it  =the  restaurant) ●'Was  the  traffic  bad?"‘Yes,it  was  terrible.'(it  =the  traffic)
●   I    wasn't   expecting    them    to   come.It(=that    they    came)was   a    complete    surprise. 请比较：
● I  don't  like this town.There's nothing to  do here.It's  a boring place. 注意there 也可以指地点“那儿”:
●   The   new    restaurant   is    very   good.I    went   there(=to    the   restaurant)last    night.
·When  we  arrived  at  the  party,there  were  already  a  lot  of  people  there(=at  the  party).
可以说 there will be, there must be,there used to be  等：
● Will there be many people at the party?
● Is there a flight to Paris this evening?”‘There might be.Ill phone the airport.'
● If people drove more carefully,there wouldn't be so many accidents. 此外：there must have been,there should have been 等：

请比较there  和it:

·There used to be a cinema in King Street but it closed a few years ago. That building is now a supermarket.It used to be a cinema.
也可以说there  is  sure/certain/likely  to  be   something( 参见第64单元E节):
● There is sure to be a flight to Paris this evening.
下面的例句要用it:
● It's dangerous to walk in the road.(It=to walk in the road) 通常以It.…开头，而不说 To walk in the road is dangerous.
● It didn't take us long to get here.(It=to get here)
● It's a pity (that)Sandra can't come to the party.(It =that Sandra can't come)
● Let's go.It's not worth waiting any longer.(It=waiting any longer) it  也可以用来表示距离、时间和天气：
●It's   a   long   way   from   here   to   the   airport.●How   far   is   it   to   the   airport?
● What day is it today?                                  ● It's a long time  since I last  saw you.
● It's going to be a nice day.                         ● It  was   windy.(但是There  was   a  cold  wind.)

some 与any
一般地说，肯定句中用some  (还有somebody/someone/something        ),否定句中用any  (还有anybody 等)。参见C 节和D 节。
在下列含有否定意思的句中要用any:
●  She went out without any money.(She didn't take any money with her.)
●  He  refused  to  eat  anything.(He  didn't  eat  anything.)
● Hardly anybody passed the examination.(=almost nobody passed)
大多数的疑问句要用any:
●  'Have  you  got  any  luggage?"'No,I  haven't.'
● 'Has anybody seen my bag?"‘Yes,it's under the table.' 期望对方作肯定回答时，some   也用于疑问句：
●What's wrong?Have you  got  something  in your  eye?(It  seems  that you have  got something in your eye and I expect you to answer ‘yes'.)
some   用于表示提供或要求的疑问句中：
● Would you like something to eat?              ●  Can  I  have  some  sugar,please?
if 从句中用any:
● If there are any letters for me,can you send them on to this address?
● If anyone has any questions,I'll be pleased to answer them.
● Let me know if you need anything.
下列句中有if  的含义，因此要用 any:
● I’m sorry for any trouble I've caused.(=if I have caused any trouble)
● Anyone who wants to do the exam must give me their names today.(=if there is anyone)
any   可以表示“无论哪个”、“任何”:
● You can catch any bus.They all go to the centre.(=it doesn't matter which bus you catch)
●‘Sing  a  song.'‘Which  song  shall  I  sing?"‘Any  song.I  don't  mind.'(=it  doesn't  matter which song)
● Come and see me any time you want.
●  'Let's  go  out  somewhere.'‘Where  shall  we  go?"‘Anywhere.I  don't  mind.'
● We left the door unlocked.Anybody could have come in.
请比较 something  与 anything:
●  A:I'm  hungry.I  want  something  to  eat.
B:What would you like?
A:I don't mind.Anything.(=something,but it doesn't matter what)
somebody/someone/anybody/anyone  是单数：
●  Someone  is  here  to  see  you.
但在这些词后常用 they/them/their:
●  Someone  has  forgotten  their  umbrella.(=his  or  her  umbrella)
●  If  anybody  wants  to   leave  early,they  can.(=he  or  she  can)
not...any→ UN185      some   o.../any o. … → UNI187       hardly any →  UNIT 10C

much time      much luck      little   energy    little   money many 和few 与名词的复数连用：
many friends      many people      few cars      few countries
a   lot   of/lots    of  /plenty   of 与不可数名词和复数名词连用：
a lot of luck                lots of time            plenty of money
a lot of friends            lots of people         plenty of ideas
plenty =more than enough:
● There's no need to hurry.We've got plenty of time.
● I've had plenty to eat.I don't want any more.
much/many     尤其用于否定句和疑问句中， a  lot  (of) 也可以这样用：
● We  didn't  spend  much  money. ( 或We  didn't  spend  a  lot  of  money.
●  Do  you  know  many  people?(或Do  you  know  a  lot  of people?)
● I don't go out much. ( 或 I don't go out a lot.)
但 a  lot  (of)更常见于肯定句。在口语中，much    不常用于肯定句：
● We  spent a  lot  of money. ( 而非We   spent   much   money)
● He  goes  out  a  lot.(而非He   goes   out   much)     many   可以用在肯定句里，但在口语中a   lot  (of) 更常见：
● A  lot  of    people ( 或 Many     people)drive     too     fast.
注意：too    much和 so    much 用于肯定句中：
● We spent too much money.
little   和few  (不带 a)   具有否定的含义(=not     much/ not    many):
●We must be quick.There is little time.(=not much,not enough time)
●He  isn't  popular.He   has  few  friends.(=not  many,not  enough  friends) 可以说very little和very few:
● There is very little time.
● He has very few friends.
a little 和a few 的含义更为肯定。 a    little=some,a small amount:
● Let's go and have a drink.We've got a little time before the train leaves. (a little time =some time,enough time to have a drink)
● 'Do you speak English?"‘A little.'(so we can talk a bit) a  few=some,a  small  number:
●I enjoy my life here.I have a few friends and we meet quite often. (a few friends=not many but enough to have a good time)
● 'When did you last see Clare?”‘A few days ago.'(=some days ago) 请比较：
●He spoke little English,so it was difficult to communicate with him.  He spoke a little English,so we were able to communicate with him.
● She's lucky.She has few problems.(=not many problems)
Things are not going so well for her.She has a few problems.(=some problems) 注意：only  a  little    和 only   a  few 具有否定的意思：
● We must be quick.We've only got a little time.
● The village was very small.There were only a few houses.

● All of us enjoyed the party. (而非everybody   of   us)
all 与 everything  比较
有时all  和 everything   可以互换：
● I'll  do  all  I  can  to  help.  或 I'll  do  everything  I  can  to  help.
通常不单独使用all,    但可以说 all  I  can  /all  you  need  等

all  还可以用来表示惟一：

every/everybody/everyone/everything  是单数，后面跟单数动词：
● Every seat in the theatre was taken.

● Everybody said they enjoyed themselves.(=he or she enjoyed himself or herself)
all与 whole 比较
whole             =complete,entire;whole 大多和单数名词连用：
● Did you read the whole book?(=all the book,not just a part of it)
● She has lived her whole life in Scotland.
whole 的前面通常有the/my/her   等。试比较 whole 与 all:
the whole book /all the book      her whole life /all her life 还可以用 a  whole..:
●   Jack   was   so   hungry,he   ate   a   whole   packet   of   biscuits.(=a   complete   packet) whole   一般不与不可数名词连用：
●  I've  spent  all  the  money  you  gave  me. (而非the   whole   money)
every/all/whole   与表示时间的词连用
every   用来表明某事发生的频率、因此说 every   day/ every  Monday/     every    ten   minutes   /every    three weeks 等：
●When  we  were  on  holiday,we  went  to  the  beach  every  day.(而非all     days)
● The bus service is very good.There's a bus every ten minutes.
● Ann gets paid every four weeks.
all day /the whole day=the complete day from beginning to end:
● We spent all ay /the whole day on the beach.
·He  was  very   quiet.He   didn't   say   a   wordall   evening/the   whole   evening. 注意 all day(而非all the day),   allweek   (而非all   the   week)等。
请比较 allthe   time 与every time:
●  They  never  go  out.They  are   at  home   all  the  time. (= always,     而非every     time)
● Every time I see you,you look different.(=each time,on every occasion)

定语从句(1)——由关系代词who/that/which
引导的从句
请看下列例句：
The woman  who lives next dooris  a  doctor.
关系从句
从句是句子的一部分，定语从句表明说话人所指的人或物：
● The  woman  who  lives  next  door. …  ( who   lives   next   door 起限定作用)
. ●  People   who    live    in    London. ( who   live   in   London  起限定作用〉 who 引导的定语从句修饰人，而不是物，who  取代了he/she/they:

再看第91单，中的例句：
·The    woman     who  lives  next  door  is  a  doctor. (或说The woman that       live...
The  woman  lives  next  door.      who  (=the    woman)是主语
·Where   is   the    cheese   that  was   in  the   fridge?(或说...the cheese  which  was...) The  cheese  was  in  the  fridge.that(=t         he  cheese)是主语
充当定语从句主语的who/that/ which   不能省略  不能说 The woman lives next door is a
doctor.  也不能说 Where is the cheese was in the fridge? 有时 who/that/which     充当定语从句中动词的宾语：
The  woman  who  I  wanted  to  see  was  away  on  holiday.
who  (=the    woman)  是宾语
I  wanted  to  see  the  woman.    I  是主语
that  (= the  keys)is  the 是宾语
You  lost  the  keys      you  是主语

the keys you lost(而非the  keys  you lost them)        the  dress  Ann  bought(而非bought    it) 注意介词(in/at/with   等)在定语从句中的位置：
do you know the woman?—Tom is talking to her   →   Do you know the woman(who/that)Tom is talking to?
the bed—I slept in it last night —wasn't very comfortable →  The bed (that/which)I slept in last night wasn't very comfortable.
●  Are  these  the  keys  (that/which)you  were  looking  for?
●  The  woman  (who/that)he  fell  in  love  with  left  him  after  a  few  weeks.
● The   man (who/that)   I  was  sitting  next to on the plane  talked  all  the  time. 上面例句中的who/that/which    都可以省略

the  books  you  were  looking  for(而非 the  books  you  were looking for  them)

●  Did  you  hear  what  they   said?(=the  things  that  they   said)

where
定语从句中的where   表示地点：
·I    recently went back to the town where I was born.(或 ….the town I was born in. 或...the town that I was born in.)
● I would like to live in a country where there is plenty of sunshine.
可以说：the    day/ the  year  / the   time(等)

●  The  last  time(that)I  saw  her,she  looked  very  well.

此外：the reason
·The reason I'm phoning you is to invite you to a party.
(或 The reason that I'm  phoning.../The  reason  why  I’m  phoning...)

定语从句分两类 比较下列例句，划线部分为定语从句：
两种类型的定语从句中都是以who 指代人，which 指代物。但是：
定语从句(类型1)→ UHITS 91-93       定语从句(类型2)→ UNT 95
EXERCISES
094.exercises:
94.1  用 who(m)/whose/which/where     将下列句子合为非限定性定语从句，从句部分可以放在句中也可以 放在句末
1 Ann is very friendly.(She lives next door.) ….Ann,who lives next door. is very friendly...
2 We stayed at the Grand Hotel.(Ann recommended it to us.)
…We stayed at the Grand Hotel.which Ann recommended to u. …

4 I went to see the doctor.(He told me to rest for a few days.)
5 John is one of my closest friends.(I have known him for a very long time.) John … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … … …          …
6 Sheila is away from home a lot.(Her job involves a lot of travelling.) ……      ……            …………………         …                     ……………
7 The new stadium…b…e…oened next month.(It can hold 90,000 people.)
..
8 We often go to visit our friends in Bristol.(It is only 30 miles away.) ………                                    ……
9 Glasgow is the largest city in Scotland.(My brother lives there.)

The strike at the car factory … … … … … … … … …      …………………………………………

I've found

7 Margaret has a son.She showed me a photograph of him.He's a policeman. Margaret showed me …………………………………………         ……………
94.3   下列句子有些可以用 which   或 that,   有些只能用which,     看示例，选择正确答案，在必要的地方加上逗号。
1   Jane  works   for   a   company   wh.ih/that     makes shoes.( 两个都是正确答案，没有逗号)
2  Colin  told  me  about  his  new  job, which/that       he's  enjoying  very  much.(只有which   是正确 答案，必须加逗号)
3  My  office  which/that  is on the second floor of the building is very small.
4  The  office  which/that  I'm using at the moment is very small.
5 She told me her address which/that  I wrote down on a piece of paper.
6 There are some words which/that are very difficult to translate.
7 The sun  which/that is one of millions of stars in the universe provides us with heat and light.
定语从句(5)——非限定性从句(2)
介词+whom/ which
在非限定性定语从句中(参见第94单元 —类 型2),介词可以用在whom   ( 修饰人)和which   ( 修饰物)之前， 因此：
to   whom/with    whom/about   which/for   which 等：
● Mr Carter,to whom I spoke on the phone last night,is very interested in our plan.
●Fortunately we had  a  map,without  which we would have  got  lost.
口语中，定语从句中的介词常常放在动词之后、这样用时，通常用who  ( 而不用whom)    来修饰人：
● This is Mr Carter,who I was telling you about.
● Yesterday we visited the City Museum,which I'd never been to before.
all of /most of 等+ whom/which 请看下列例句：
同样地，可以说：
none of /neither of /any of /either of
some of/many of /much of/(a) few of
both of /half of /each of /one of /two of (等)
+whom(people) +which (things)
● Tom tried on three jackets,none of which fitted him.
● Two men,neither of whom I had ever seen before,came into my office.
● They've got three cars,two of which they never use.
● Sue has a lot of friends,many of whom she was at school with.
which  (不用what)
请看下列例句：
Jim passed his driving test.    This      surprised      everybody. (  两个句子)
Jim passed his driving test,   which    surprised      everybody. ( 一个句子) 	   定语从句
本例中，which=t  he fact that he passed his  driving    test。在下列句子中，必须用which   (不能说 what):
·   Sheila  couldn't  come  to  the  party,which  was  a  pity.(而非 ..what was a pity)
● The weather was very good, which  we  hadn’t   expected. (而非...what   we   hadn't expected)
关于what,    另参见第91单元C 节和第92单元D 节。
all of/none of 等 → UHIT 87        both of 等 → UNIT 88       定语从句(1)-(4)→  UHITS 91-94

UNIT
-ing 与 -ed 短语比较(the woman talking to Tom, the boy injured in the accident)
短语是句子的一部分， 一些短语以-ing  或 -ed  开头：
the woman
Do you know the woman talking to Tom? L -ing 短语 一
The boy injured in the accident was taken to hospital.
the boy injured in the accident
- ing   短语表明某人在特定的时间正在做某事：
● Do you know the woman talking to Tom?(the woman is talking to Tom)
● The police investigating the crime are looking for three men.(the police are investigating the crime)
· Who were those people waiting outside?(they were waiting)
● Iwas woken up by a bell ringing.(a bell was ringing)
谈论事物或人时， -ing 短语表示一般的事实：
● The road joining the two villages is very narrow.(the road joins the two villages)
● Ilive in a pleasant room overlooking the garden.(the room overlooks the garden)
· Can you think of the name of a flower beginning with ^T?(the name begins with T)
-ed  短语有被动含义：
● The boy injured in the accident was taken to hospital.(the boy was injured in the accident)
● Some  of the  people  invited  to  the  party  can't  come.(the  people  have  been  invited  to  the party )
injured     和 invited      是过去分词， 但也有很多动词的过去分词不以-ed  结尾(如made,bought,stolen             等):
● Most of the goods made in this factory are exported.(the goods are made...)
· The   police    never   found    the   money    stolen    in   the    robbery.(the    money   was    stolen) left   也可以这样用，意为“没用完”、“剩余”:
· We've  spent  nearlyall our money.We've only got a little left.
不规则动词的过去分词，参见附录1。
there   is   /there   was  等后面常常用-ing 和 -ed  短语：
● There were some children swimming in the river.
● Is there anybody waiting?
● There was a big red car parked outside the house.
192         see/hear somebody doing something→LUNIT 66                                there (is)→ UNIT 83

UNIT
以-ing 和-ed 结尾的形容词(boring/bored等)比较
许多形容词以-ing  和-ed  结尾，如boring  和bored 。 请看下列例句：
Jane has been doing the same job for a very long time. Every day she does exactly the same thing again and again.She doesn't enjoy it any more and would like to do something different.
Jane's job is boring.
Jane is bored(with her job).
Somebody is bored if something(or somebody else)is boring.Or,if something is boring,it make s you bored.    这就表明了两者的区别。所以说：
·Jane is bored because her job is boring.
● Jane's   job    is    boring,so   Jane    is    bored.(而非Jane     is    boring)
If  a    person    is    boring, this    means    that    they   make    other    people     bored   .  这就表明了两者的区别。 所以说：
●George always talks about the same things.He's really boring.
请比较以-ing   和-ed 结尾的形容词：

有时两个或两个以上的形容词可以连用：

形容词，如 new/arge/round/       wooden,     是“事实”形容词，表明物体的大小、颜色、新旧等。 而 nice/beautiful      等形容词表明说话人的观点
a     tall     young     man(1→2)                                a      large      wooden     table(1→5)
big     blue      eyes(1→3)                                        an      old     Russian      song(2→4)
a     small      black      plastic     bag(1→3→5)        an      old      white      cotton      shirt(2→3→5)
表示大小、长度的形容词(big/small/tall   /short/long     等 )一般放在表示形状和宽度的形容词之前(round/fat/thin/ slim/ wide   等):
a  large  round  table         a  tall  thin   girl          a   long   narrow    street
两个表明颜色的形容词连用时，两词之间用and  连接：
a   black   and   white   dress          a    red,white    and   green    flag
但要说  a   long   black    dress(而非a long and black dress)
可以说  the  first  two  days,the  next  few  weeks, the  last  ten  minutes   等：
·I    didn't    enjoy the  first  two  days  of the  course.( 而非 the  two  first  days)
●   They'll   be   away    for   the  next   few  weeks. ( 而非the    few   next    weeks)
D       be/get/become/seem 后面跟形容词
●   Be    careful!                                                              ●   I'm    tired    and   I'm    getting    hungry.
●   As   the    film   went   on,it   became   more    and   more   boring.
●   Your   friend   seems   very   nice.
形容词也用来补充说明 look ,feel,    sound,      taste 或 smell:
●    You    look    tired./I     feel    tired./She    sounds    tired.
●   The   dinner   smells   good.                                   ●   This  tea  tastes   a   bit   strange.
但是谈论某人做某事的方式时，则要用副词(参见第99-100单元):
●   Drive   carefully!   (而非Drive       careful)
●   Susan   plays   the   piano  very  well. (而非plays...very       good)

形容词与副词比较(1)(quick/quickly)
请看下列例句：
● Our holiday was too short-the time went very quickly.
●  The  driver  of  the  car  was  seriously  injured  in  the  accident. quickl y 和 seriously   是副词  很多副词由形容词+-ly 构成：
形容词： quick           serious        careful         quiet        heavy        bad
副词：   quickly       seriously       carefully     quietly     heavily      badly
有关拼写规则，参见附录6
并不是所有以-ly 结尾的词都是副词， 一些形容词结尾也是-1y:
friendly    lively      elderly     lonely       silly      lovely
是形容词还是副词?
副词也可以放在形容词或一些副词之前：
reasonably cheap         (副词+形容词)
terribly sorry                (副词+形容词)
incredibly quickly         (副词+副词)
● It's a reasonably cheap restaurant and the food is extremely good.
●    Oh, I'm  terribly   sorry.I  didn't  mean  to  push  you.(而非terible  sorry)
● Maria learns languages incredibly quickly.
● The examination was surprisingly easy.
副词也可以用在过去分词(injured/organised/written   等)之前：
Two  people were  seriously  injured  in  the  accident.( 而非serious    injured)
The  meeting  was  very  badly  organised.

形容词与副词比较(2)(well/fast/late,hard/hardly)
good/well
good    是形容词，well  是副词：
● Your   English   is   good.         但要说   You    speak    English    well.
● Susan  is  a    good    pianist.      但要说   Susan    plays    the   piano    well.
well  与过去分词连用(dressed/known     等):
well-dressed      well-known       well-educated      well-paid well  也可以用作形容词，意思是“健康的”:
● 'How   are   you   today?''I'm   very   well,thanks.'(而非I’m very  good)
fast/hard/late
这些词既是形容词也是副词：
形容词                     副词
● Jack is a very fast runner.        Jack can run very  fast.
● Ann is a hard worker.              Ann works hard. (而非works hardly)
● The train was late.                   I got up late this morning.
lately=recently
● Have you seen Tom lately?
hardly
hardly=   very    little,almost     not。请看下列例句：
● Sarah was rather unfriendly to me at the party.She hardly spoke to me. (=she spoke to me very little,almost not at all)
● George and Hilda want to get married but they've only known each other for a few days.I don't think they  should get married yet.They hardly know  each other.
(= they know each other very little)
hard 和hardly   不同。试比较：
● He tried hard to find a job but he had no luck.(=he tried a lot,with a lot of effort)
● I'm not surprised he didn't find a job.He hardly tried to find one.(=he tried very little) 可以用hardly+any/anybody/anyone/anything/anywhere:
● A:How much money have you got?
B:Hardly  any.(=very  little,almost  none)
● I'll have to go shopping.We've got hardly any food.
● The exam results were very bad.Hardly anybody 	in our class passed.(=very few students passed,  almost nobody passed)
● She ate hardly anything.She wasn't feeling
hungry.(=she ate very little,almost nothing)
注意 hardly  的位置：
● She ate hardly anything.    或  She  hardly  ate  anything.
● We've got hardly any food.    或  We've hardly got any food.
可以说 can/could+hardly.I  can  hardly do something = it's almost impossible for me to do it:
● Your writing is terrible.I can hardly read it.(=it is almost impossible for me to read it)
● My leg was hurting me.I could hardly walk.
hardly ever=almost never
● I'm nearly always at home in the evenings.I hardly ever go out.

● She ate hardly anything.    或  She  hardly  ate  anything.
● We've got hardly any food.    或  We've hardly got any food.
可以说 can/could+hardly.I  can  hardly do something = it's almost impossible for me to do it:
● Your writing is terrible.I can hardly read it.(=it is almost impossible for me to read it)
● My leg was hurting me.I could hardly walk.
hardly ever=almost never
● I'm nearly always at home in the evenings.I hardly ever go out.

enough   用在形容词和副词之后：
● He didn't get the job because he wasn't  experienced    enough. (而非enough      experienced)
● You won’t pass the examination if you don't work hard enough.
●    She    shouldn't    get    married    yet.She's   not    old    enough.
反意词是 too …   (too   hard   /too    old 等):
●  You  never  stop  working.You  work  too  hard.
enough  一般用在名词之前：
● He didn't get the job because he didn't have enough experience.(而非experience enough)
● I'd like to go away on holiday but I haven't got enough money.
● Some of us had to sit on the floor because there weren't enough chairs. enough  还可以单独使用(不加名词):
●I'lllend you  some  money  if you  haven't  got  enough.
反意词是 too much.../    too  many..:
●We can't go away on holiday.It costs too much(money).
● There are too many people and not enough chairs.
可以说 enough/to...for                  (somebody/something):
●  I  haven't  got  enough  money  for  a  holiday.
● He wasn't experienced enough for the job.
● This shirt is too big for me.I need a smaller size.
但通常用enough/too...to do something(而非for       doing)。因此可以说：
enough    money  to  buy  something       too   young  to  do   something  etc.
例如：
● I haven't  got enough money to go on holiday.( 而非for   going)
● He wasn't experienced enough to do the job.
● She's  not  old enough to  get married.       或者  She's  too  young  to   get    married.
● Let's get a taxi.It's too far to walk home from here.
● There weren't enough chairs for everyone to sit down.
● They spoke too quickly for us to understand.
可以说：
其他一些例句如下：
● The wallet was too big to put in my pocket. (而非too big  to put  it)
●  These  boxes  are  too  heavy  to  carry.(而非too heavy  to   carry  them)
● The water wasn't clean enough to swim in.

(与比较级的规则相同。参见第104单元)
long→longest            hot  →hottest           easy→easiest            hard→hardest
但     most famous              most boring               most difficult            most expensive
不规则形容词的最高级：
good→best                 bad→worst              far→furthest
拼写法规则参见附录6
在最高级形式之前要用定冠词the(the  longest  / the   most    famous 等):

最高级形式之后常使用动词的现在完成时(另参见第10单元A节):

107.title: 最 高 级 — the     longest/ the   most   enjoyable  等
107.explanation:
1  I  can't  reach  as  high  as  you.You   are   taller ….han me. …
