6 They've been very lucky.I wish we were as lucky …………………………………
最高级——the longest/the most enjoyable等
请看下列例句：
最高级是在原级后加-est  或原级前加most … 。  通常，单音节的形容词或副词的最高级形式或少数双 音节的形容词或副词的最高级形式在原级后加-est 。 双音节或多音节的形容词或副词原级前加most。
(与比较级的规则相同。参见第104单元)
long→longest            hot  →hottest           easy→easiest            hard→hardest
但     most famous              most boring               most difficult            most expensive
不规则形容词的最高级：
good→best                 bad→worst              far→furthest
拼写法规则参见附录6
在最高级形式之前要用定冠词the(the  longest  / the   most    famous 等):
● Yesterday was the hottest day of the year.
● That film was really boring.It was the most boring film I've ever seen.
● She is a really nice person —one of the nicest people I know.
● Why does he always come to see me at the worst possible moment? 请比较：
● This  hotel  is  the  cheapest  in  town.(最高级)
●   This   hotel   is cheaper    than   all    the   others    in   town. ( 比较级)
oldest 与eldest 比较
old  的最高级形式是oldest:
●   That   church   is the oldest  building   in   the   town.( 而非the      eldest) 谈论家人时用eldest  (或oldest):
● My eldest  son is  13  years  old.(或 My  oldest  son.…)
● Are you the  eldest  in  your  family?(或 ...  the    oldest...)
最高级形式之后表示地点(城市、建筑等)的词之前要用介词in:
● What is the longest river  in  the  world?(而非of  the  world)
·We     had,a     lovely   room. It  was  one  of the  nicest  in the  hotel.( 而非of   the   hotel) 在表示组织和人群的集体名词(班/队/公司等)之前也要用介词in:
● Who  is  the  best   student  in  the   class?(  而非of  the  class') of  一般用于表示一段时间：
● What was the happiest day of your life?
● Yesterday was the hottest day of the year.
最高级形式之后常使用动词的现在完成时(另参见第10单元A节):
● What's the best film you've ever seen?
● That was the most delicious meal I've had for a long time.
有时用most+    形容词表示“非常”的意思：
● The  book  you  lent  me  was  most  interesting.   (非常有趣)
● Thank  you  for  the  money.It  was  most  generous  of  you.(非常慷慨)

词序(2)——副词与动词
有些副词(如always,also,probably )在句中间与动词连用：
● Tom always goes to work by car.
·We were feeling very tired and we were also hungry.
● Your car has probably been stolen.
请看下列副词在句中位置的规则(它们只是普通规则，有许多例外)
i)如果动词是一个词(如goes/fel/cooked等),那么副词放在动词之前：
●  I  cleaned  the  house  and  also  cooked  the dinner.   ( 而非cooked      also)
● Lucy hardly ever watches television and rarely reads newspapers. 注意下列副词( always/often/also 等)放在have  to 之前：
●We  always  have  to  wait  a  long  time  for  the  bus.(而非we  have  always  to  wait)
ii)    副词用在am/is/are/was/were    之后：
● We were  feeling very tired  and we were also hungry.
●  Why   are   you   always   late?You're   never   on   time.
● The traffic isn't usually as bad as it was this morning.
如果谓语动词由两个或两个以上的词组成(can   remember   / doesn't     smoke/has      been      stolen 等), 副词放在第一个动词(can/doesn't/has        之后):
●My  parents have always lived  in London.
● Jack can't cook.He can't even boil an egg.
● The house was only built a year ago and it's already falling down. 注意probably    用在否定词之前。因此可以说：
● I probably  won't   see  you. 或 I  willprobably   not  see  you.( 而非I     won't    probably..)
all  和both  还可以用在下列位置：
● We all felt illafter   the    meal. ( 而非we    felt    allill)
·My   parents    are    both teachers. ( 而非my  parents  both  are  teachers)
● Sarah and Jane have both applied for the job.
● We  are  allgoing out this evening.
有时使用is/will/did 等避免句子的重复(参见第50单元A节)。注意always/never    等在下列句中的位置：
·He always says he won't be late but he always is.(=he is always late)
●  Ive  never   done  it  and  I  never  will.(=I   will  never   do  it) 在这类句子中，always/never     等一般放在动词之前。

still,yet与already比较 any more/any longer/no longer
still
still   用于表示情况或动作持续进行着，没有变化或停止：
● It's 10 o'clock and Tom is still in bed.
● When I went to bed,Jane was still working.
●  Do  you   still  want  to   go  to  the  party   or  have  you   changed  your  mind? still  通常与动词在句子中间连用 参见第109单元
any   more/any   longer/no   longer
not. .any    more  或 not…any     longer  用于表示情况已发生了变化  any    more 和 any    longer 用在句子的结尾部分：
●  Ann  doesn't  work  here any more ( 或any  longer).She  left  last  month. (而非Ann  doesn't  still  work  here)
● We used  to  be  good  friends  but  we  aren't  any  more ( 或 any   longer). 还可以把 no  longer 放在句子中间：
·Ann   no   longer   works   here.
注意no   more 一般不这样使用：
● We  are  no  longer  friends. (而非We   are  no  more   friends) 请比较still 与not..any     more:
● Sheila still works here but Ann doesn't work here any more.
yet
yet 表示“到目前为止”yet 主要用在否定句(I haven't  finished  yet)和疑问句(Have  you  finished yet? )中  yet 表示说话人期待某事发生，通常用在句子的结尾部分：
yet
● It's 10 o'clock and Tom hasn't got up yet.

still
still   用于表示情况或动作持续进行着，没有变化或停止：
● It's 10 o'clock and Tom is still in bed.
● When I went to bed,Jane was still working.

not. .any    more  或 not…any     longer  用于表示情况已发生了变化  any    more 和 any    longer 用在句子的结尾部分：

yet 表示“到目前为止”yet 主要用在否定句(I haven't  finished  yet)和疑问句(Have  you  finished yet? )中  yet 表示说话人期待某事发生，通常用在句子的结尾部分：

·We're going to the beach tomorrow.It doesn't matter what the weather is like.We're going to the beach even if it's raining.
·We hope to go to the beach tomorrow,but we won't go if it's raining

as (原因与时间)
as ( 表示原因)
as   有时的含义是“因为”:
● As it was a public holiday,all the  shops were shut.(因为这是节日)
● As they live near us,we see them quite often.
●    We    watched    television    all    evening    as    we    had    nothing    better    to    do. as   还用于表示两件事同时发生 参见 B  节
as  ( 表 示 原 因 )
as  可以用于表示两件事同时发生：
●  I  watched  her  as  she  opened  the  letter. ( I    watched 与 she   opened   在同一时间)
● As they walked along the street,they looked in the shop windows.
●   Can   you  turn  off  the  light  as  you   go  out, please?  ( 在出门时) 也可以说，正在做某事时，另一件事发生了：
● Jill slipped as she was getting off the bus.
● The thief was seen as he was climbing over the wall. as常用于表示两个短暂的动作同时发生：
● George   arrived    as   Sue    left. ( 他到的同时苏离开)
● We all waved goodbye to Liz as she drove away in her car.
但as  还用于表示两件事同时发生了一段时间：
● As the day went on,the weather got worse.
● I began to enjoy the job more as I got used to it. 还可以用 just   as (在某一确切的时间):
●Just  as  I  sat  down,the  phone  rang.
●Just as we were going out,it started to rain.
● I had to leave just as the conversation was getting interesting. 过去进行时(was     getting/were     going 等)参见第6单元。
as,when   与 while 比较
as 用于表示两件事同时发生，而 when  表示一件事紧接另一件事发生。请比较when  与 as:
● When  I  got  home,I  had  a  bath. ( 而非as  I  got  home)
● As  I  walked  into  the  room,the  phone   started  ringing.(在同时间)
as (时间)用于说明动作和行为。 as    +一种情况或状态(不是动作)通常用来表示“因为”(参见 A节):
● As we were asleep,we  didn't  hear  the   doorbell. (因为我们睡觉了)
●   As   they    live   near    me, I    see   them    quite    often. (因为他们住得离我很近) 下列句子中不能用as  表示时间，而要用while    或 when:
● The     doorbellrang while we were asleep.(而非as we were asleep)
● Angela   got    married  when  she  was  23.( 而非as   she   was   23)

116.title: like 与 as 比较
116.explanation:

116  like 与 as 比较
like  表示“与……相似” 注意，下列句中不可以用as:
●  What  a  beautiful  house!  It's  like  a  palace.(而非as    a   palace)
●  'What  does   Sandra  do?"      'She's   a   teacher,like  me '(而非as   me)
● Be  careful!The  floor  has  been  polished. It's  like  walking  on  ice. (而非as walking)
●  It's  raining   again. I  hate weather  like  this. (而非as this)
在这些句子中，like  是介词，后面加名词(like  a   palace)、代词(like    me/like    this) 或动词的-ing 形式(like     walking)
还可以说 like(somebody/something)doing                   something:
● 'What's that noise?''It  sounds like a baby crying.'
有时like  表示“像……那样”:
●    Some    sports,like   motor   racing,can   be   dangerous.
还可以用such   as (表示“如”):
● Some sports,such as motor racing,can be dangerous.
as  ( 而非like)    用在主语+动词之前：
● I didn't move anything.I left everything as I found it.
●  They  did  as    they   promised. (他们所做的像他们所保证的一样) 比较下列句中like 与as:
● You  should have  done  it  like  this.(like+   代词)
● You  should have  done it  as I  showed you. (as+  主语+动词) 还可以说as  you  know/as  I  said  / as  she  expected/as I thought 等：
●  As  you  know,it's  Tom's  birthday   next  week. (你已知道)
● Jane   failed   her   driving   test,as   she   expected.(她以前已想到) 注意可以说as    usual/as     always:
● You're late as usual.
as   也可以用作介词，但与 like   含义不同。试比较：
as (介词)表示“作为”、“以……身份”等：

● London is all right as a place to visit,but I wouldn't like to live there.
● The news of her death came as a great shock.
可以说regard... as:

for,during 与 while 比较
for  与during   比较
用for+    一段时间表示时间的持续：
for two hours                             for ages
例如 ：
● We watched television for two hours last night.
·Victoria is going away for a week in September.
● Where have you been?I've been waiting for ages.
·Are  you  going  away  for  the  weekend?
用during+  名词表示事情发生的时间，不讲多长时间：
during  the   film   during   our   holiday   during  the  night 例如：
● I fell asleep during the film.
● We met a lot of people  during our holiday.
● The ground is wet.It must have rained during the night.
表示时间的词(例如the    morning/the     afternoon    /the     summer)通常与in  或during   连用：
●  It  must  have  rained  in  the  night. (或…. during  the  night.)
·    I'll  phone  you   sometime during the afternoon.( 或...in  the afternoon.) 不能用during   来表示时间的持续：
· It   rained   for   three   days   without   stopping.( 而非during     three    days) 请比较 during   与 for:
·I fell asleep during the film.I was asleep for half an hour.
during 与while 比较 请比较：
再看几个用while   的例句：
●We saw Amanda while we were waiting for the bus.
● While you were out,there was a phone call for you.
·Christopher  read  a  book  while  I  watched  television.
在while   之后用一般现在时(不是将来时)表示将来：
●I'll be in London next week.I hope to  see Tom while I’m there.(而非while I will be there)
·W    hat  are  you  going  to do while you are waiting? ( 而非while  you  will  be  waiting) 另参见第25单元。
for与 since比较→UNI 12B       whle-ing→UW167B

by 与 until比较 by the time...
by(+       表 示 时 间 的 词 ) 表 示 “ 不 晚 于 … … " ”
●    I    posted    the     letter    today,so    they     should    receive it    by    Monday.(在星期一或之前，不晚于星期一)
● We'd better hurry.We have to be at home by 5 o'clock. (在5点或之前，不晚于5点)
●  Where's  Sue?She  should  be  here  by  now. (在现在或之前，因此她应该已经到了)
This cheese should be used by 14 August.
不能用until   表示下列意义：
● Tell me by Friday whether or not you can come  to  the  party. ( 而非Tell   me   until  Friday)
用until  ( 或till) 来表示情况的持续：
● 'Shall    we    go    now?’      'No  ,let's      wait     until ( 或till)it        stops       raining.'
I   stayed   in   bed   until   half   past   ten.
●    I     couldn't     get    up    this    morning.
I    didn't    get    up    until    half    past    ten.
请比较until  与by:
可以说 by   the   time   something   happens,     请看下列例句：
●  It's  not  worth  going  shopping  now.By  the  time  we  get  to  the  shops,they  will  be  closed. (商店在现在与我们到的时候之间会关门)
● (在信中)I’m  flying  to  the  United  States  this   evening.So   by   the   time   you   receive   this letter, I'll  be   in  New   York. (我在现在与你接到信的时候之间抵达纽约)
● Hurry up!By the time we get to the cinema,the film will already have started. 可以说 by  the  time  something  happened  (指过去):
·Jane's   car   broke   down   on   the   way   to   the   party   last   night.By   the   time   she   arrived,most of  the   other  guests  had  gone. (她用了很长时间才到聚会的地方，但在这期间大部分客人都   回家了)
●  I  had   a  lot  of  work  to   do  yesterday   evening.I   was   very   tired   by   the   time   I    finished. (工 作花去我很长时间，在工作期间我越来越疲倦)
· We went to the cinema last night.It took us a long time to find somewhere to park the car.By the time we got to the cinema,the film had already started.
还可以用by then 或 by that time:
·Jane  finally  arrived  at  the  party  at   midnight,but    by   then ( 或by  that  time),most   of  the guests  had  gone.

120.title: at/on/in(时间)
120.explanation:
1 Jane was invited to a party but she got there much later than she intended.
By the time she got to the part...,most of the other guests had gone.
2 I had to catch a train but it took me longer than expected to get to the station.
………                 …     …  …,my train had already gone.
3 I saw two men who looked as if they were trying to steal a car.I called the police but it was some time before they arrived.
……     ……,the   two    men   had   disappeared.
4  A  man   escaped  from  prison   last  night.  It was a  long  time  before  the  guards  discovered  what had     happened.
……………,the escaped prisoner was miles away.
5 I intended to go shopping after finishing my work. But I finished my work much later than expected.     ……………………… ……………………………       …,it was too late to go shopping.
at  用于下列短语：
注意疑问句通常用What time...?(通常不用At what time..?):
● What time are you going out this evening?
可以说：
in the morning(s)      in  the  afternoon(s)      in the evening(s)
● I'll see you in the morning.                      ●  Do  you work  in the evenings? 但要说
on Friday morning(s)      on Sunday afternoon(s)      on Monday evening(s)等
● I'llbe at home on Friday morning.        ●  Do  you usually go out on Saturday evenings?
last/next/thislevery  前不用at/on/in:
● P'll see you next Friday.(而非on next  Friday)
● They got married last March.
in   a   few   minutes/in    six   months  等表示将来某时：
● The  train  will  be  leaving  in  a  few  minutes.(从现在开始的几分钟后)
·J    ack   has   gone   away.He'llbe   back   in   a   week.(从现在开始的一周后)
● She'llbe here in a moment.(一会儿)
还可以说 in six months'time,in  a  week's   time  等：
● They're   getting   married   in   six  months'time. (或...in  six  months.) 还可以用in 表示“用……时间”:
● I  learnt  to   drive  in  four  weeks.(我学驾驶用了四周时间)
on/n time,at/n the ond →(UkI1121

短语动词(get up/ break down/fill in 等)
下列词常在动词后使用：
因此可以说 put   out   /get   on /take  off  /run  away 等，它们被称为短语动词。
out/off/up 等常与表示移动的动词一起使用：
get on           ● The bus was full.We couldn't get on.
drive off       ·A woman got into the car and drove off.
come   back·Sally   is   leaving   tomorrow   and   coming   back   on   Saturday.
turn   round   ·When   I   touched   him   on   the   shoulder,he   turned   round.
但通常是out/off/up   等词赋予了动词特殊的含义：
break      down ·  Sorry  I'm  late.The  car   broke     down. (机器坏了)
look   out        ● Look    out! There's  a  car  coming. ( 注意)
take  off        ●  It  was  my  first  flight. I  was  nervous  as  the plane took off. (起飞)
get  up           ●  I  was  very  tired  this  morning. I   couldn't  get  up. (起床)
get  on            ● How  was  the  exam? How  did  you  get  on?(你考得怎样?)
get by            ·My   French  isn't  very  good  but   it's  enough  to get by.(饶幸成功)
B     有时短语动词后面使用介词：
短语动词  介词
run away     from
keep   up   with look  forward    to
cut down    on
● Why did you run away from me?
·You're walking too fast.I can’t keep up with you.
● Are you looking forward to your holiday?
● Jack  is trying to cut down on   smoking.( 戒烟)
C    有时短语动词有宾语，在这种情况下，宾语在句中位置有下列两种：
可以说：
如果宾语是代词(it/them/me/him 等),那么它在句中的位置只有下面一种：
I   turned          it           off.   ( 而非I  turned   off  it)

UNIT
短语动词(get up/ break down/fill in 等)
下列词常在动词后使用：
因此可以说 put   out   /get   on /take  off  /run  away 等，它们被称为短语动词。
out/off/up 等常与表示移动的动词一起使用：
get on           ● The bus was full.We couldn't get on.
drive off       ·A woman got into the car and drove off.
come   back·Sally   is   leaving   tomorrow   and   coming   back   on   Saturday.
turn   round   ·When   I   touched   him   on   the   shoulder,he   turned   round.
但通常是out/off/up   等词赋予了动词特殊的含义：
break      down ·  Sorry  I'm  late.The  car   broke     down. (机器坏了)
look   out        ● Look    out! There's  a  car  coming. ( 注意)
take  off        ●  It  was  my  first  flight. I  was  nervous  as  the plane took off. (起飞)
get  up           ●  I  was  very  tired  this  morning. I   couldn't  get  up. (起床)
get  on            ● How  was  the  exam? How  did  you  get  on?(你考得怎样?)
get by            ·My   French  isn't  very  good  but   it's  enough  to get by.(饶幸成功)
B     有时短语动词后面使用介词：
短语动词  介词
run away     from
keep   up   with look  forward    to
cut down    on
● Why did you run away from me?
·You're walking too fast.I can’t keep up with you.
● Are you looking forward to your holiday?
● Jack  is trying to cut down on   smoking.( 戒烟)
C    有时短语动词有宾语，在这种情况下，宾语在句中位置有下列两种：
可以说：
如果宾语是代词(it/them/me/him 等),那么它在句中的位置只有下面一种：
I   turned          it           off.   ( 而非I  turned   off  it)
再看一些例句：
● Could
They  gave  me  a  form  and  told  me  to  fillit  in.(而非 fill  in  it)
● The police got into the house by
The door wasn't locked.Why did the police break it down?(而非 break  down  it)
● I think I'll
Do   you  want  these  newspapers  or  shall  I  hrow  them  away? (而非 throw     away     them)
●   Don''t
The   baby    is    asleep.Don't   wake    her    up. ( 而非 wake    up     her)
